name: 2WikiMultihopQA
intro: 2WikiMultiHopQA是一个多跳问答数据集，旨在通过要求模型阅读多个段落以回答问题来测试推理和推断能力。该数据集的独特之处在于它为多跳问题提供了包含推理路径的证据信息，这既能为模型的预测提供全面的解释，也有助于评估模型的推理能力。数据集的构建利用了结构化（Wikidata）和非结构化数据，并通过精心设计的流程和模板来保证问题的质量和多跳推理的必要性。
build_method: 混合, 自动生成
eval_feature: Multi-hop, 长文本, 过程导向
intro_en: 2WikiMultiHopQA is a multi-hop question answering dataset designed to test reasoning and inference abilities by requiring models to read multiple paragraphs to answer questions. Its uniqueness lies in providing evidence containing reasoning paths for multi-hop questions, which not only offers comprehensive explanations for model predictions but also helps evaluate the model's reasoning capabilities. The dataset is constructed using both structured (Wikidata) and unstructured data, with carefully designed processes and templates to ensure question quality and the necessity of multi-hop reasoning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Wikipedia Articles) --> B[Information Extraction]
    B --> C[Multi-hop Path Identification]
    C --> D[Question Generation]
    D --> E[Answer & Evidence Generation]
    E --> F{{Automated Generation Tool}}
    F --> G[Automated Quality Checks]
    G --> H{Quality Threshold Met?}
    H -- Yes --> I[Human Review & Refinement]
    H -- No --> J[Discard / Re-generate]
    I --> K(2WikiMultihopQA Dataset)
    K --> L[Model Evaluation]
    L --> M[Performance Metrics]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Extract structured data from Wikidata knowledge base"] --> B["Apply logical rules to structured data to identify multi-hop reasoning paths"]
    B["Apply logical rules to structured data to identify multi-hop reasoning paths"] --> C["Generate question-answer pairs using designed templates that guarantee multi-hop steps"]
    C["Generate question-answer pairs using designed templates that guarantee multi-hop steps"] --> D["Retrieve corresponding Wikipedia paragraphs as unstructured data for each question"]
    D["Retrieve corresponding Wikipedia paragraphs as unstructured data for each question"] --> E["Annotate evidence information containing comprehensive reasoning paths for each sample"]
    E["Annotate evidence information containing comprehensive reasoning paths for each sample"] --> F["Validate that questions require multi-hop reasoning and cannot be answered trivially"]
    F["Validate that questions require multi-hop reasoning and cannot be answered trivially"] --> G["Evaluate multi-hop models on answer prediction and reasoning path correctness"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从Wikidata知识库提取结构化数据"] --> B["对结构化数据应用逻辑规则识别多跳推理路径"]
    B["对结构化数据应用逻辑规则识别多跳推理路径"] --> C["使用设计模板生成保证多跳步骤的问答对"]
    C["使用设计模板生成保证多跳步骤的问答对"] --> D["为每个问题检索对应的维基百科段落作为非结构化数据"]
    D["为每个问题检索对应的维基百科段落作为非结构化数据"] --> E["为每个样本标注包含完整推理路径的证据信息"]
    E["为每个样本标注包含完整推理路径的证据信息"] --> F["验证问题需要多跳推理且无法简单回答"]
    F["验证问题需要多跳推理且无法简单回答"] --> G["评估多跳模型的答案预测和推理路径正确性"]

name: AA-LCR
intro: AA-LCR包含100个基于文本的难题，需要跨多个真实世界文档进行推理，每个文档集平均约100k输入tokens。问题设计旨在使答案不能直接从文档中检索，而必须从多个信息源进行推理。
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Knowledge QA, Math Reasoning
intro_en: AA-LCR consists of 100 text-based challenges requiring reasoning across multiple real-world documents, with each document set averaging about 100k input tokens. The questions are designed so that answers cannot be directly retrieved from the documents but must be inferred from multiple information sources.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Documents) --> B[Expert Challenge Design]
    B --> C[Document Selection & Integration]
    C --> D[Complex Question Generation]
    D --> E[Manual Annotation]
    E --> F{{Hybrid Construction Methods}}
    F --> G{Quality Assurance Check}
    G -- Fail --> H[Re-annotation & Revision]
    H --> E
    G -- Pass --> I[Final AA-LCR Dataset]
    I --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Performance Metrics)

name: AA-Omniscience
intro: AA-Omniscience是一个旨在衡量大型语言模型跨领域知识可靠性的基准。它包含6000个问题，涵盖42个经济相关主题，评估模型的事实召回能力和知识校准。该基准通过全知指数（Omniscience Index）来衡量模型表现，该指数会惩罚幻觉并奖励不确定时的弃权。评估结果揭示了前沿模型在事实性和校准方面的持续弱点。
intro_en: AA-Omniscience is a benchmark designed to measure the cross-domain knowledge reliability of large language models. It contains 6,000 questions covering 42 economics-related topics, evaluating models' factual recall and knowledge calibration. The benchmark uses the Omniscience Index to assess performance, which penalizes hallucinations and rewards abstentions when uncertain. Evaluation results reveal persistent weaknesses in state-of-the-art models regarding factual accuracy and calibration.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[(42 Economics Topics)] --> B[Expert Knowledge Acquisition]
    B --> C[Question Generation]
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E{Quality Control & Validation}
    E -- Fail --> F[Refine & Re-annotate]
    F --> E
    E -- Pass --> G(AA-Omniscience Dataset
6,000 Questions)
    G --> H[LLM Evaluation]
    H --> I{{Omniscience Index}}
    I --> J(Factual Recall & Calibration Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select 42 topics across six economically important domains requiring factual precision"] --> B["Gather facts from authoritative, up-to-date primary and secondary sources"]
    B["Gather facts from authoritative, up-to-date primary and secondary sources"] --> C["Generate questions using automated agent from source facts"]
    C["Generate questions using automated agent from source facts"] --> D["Filter and revise questions for difficulty, ambiguity, source-reliance, and precision"]
    D["Filter and revise questions for difficulty, ambiguity, source-reliance, and precision"] --> E["Prompt models to answer only if confident, abstain otherwise, without context or tools"]
    E["Prompt models to answer only if confident, abstain otherwise, without context or tools"] --> F["Grade answers using Gemini 2.5 Flash as correct, partially correct, incorrect, or not attempted"]
    F["Grade answers using Gemini 2.5 Flash as correct, partially correct, incorrect, or not attempted"] --> G["Compute Omniscience Index as 100·(c-i)/(c+p+i+a) to penalize hallucinations and reward abstention"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["选择六个经济重要领域的42个主题"] --> B["从权威的最新一手和二手来源收集事实"]
    B["从权威的最新一手和二手来源收集事实"] --> C["使用自动化代理从来源事实生成问题"]
    C["使用自动化代理从来源事实生成问题"] --> D["筛选并修订问题的难度、歧义、来源依赖和精确性"]
    D["筛选并修订问题的难度、歧义、来源依赖和精确性"] --> E["提示模型仅在有信心时回答，否则弃权，无上下文或工具"]
    E["提示模型仅在有信心时回答，否则弃权，无上下文或工具"] --> F["使用Gemini 2.5 Flash将答案分为正确、部分正确、错误或未尝试"]
    F["使用Gemini 2.5 Flash将答案分为正确、部分正确、错误或未尝试"] --> G["计算全知指数100·(c-i)/(c+p+i+a)以惩罚幻觉并奖励弃权"]

name: ACPBench Hard
intro: ACPBench Hard是ACPBench的困难版本，用于评估大型语言模型在复杂推理和规划任务中的能力。该基准包含更具挑战性的任务，要求模型进行多步推理和复杂的动作规划。
build_method: 蒸馏出的核心推理任务, 生成式问题格式
eval_feature: 多步推理, 规划
build_method_en: distilled eight core reasoning tasks, generative question formats
intro_en: ACPBench Hard is the challenging version of ACPBench, designed to evaluate large language models' abilities in complex reasoning and planning tasks. This benchmark includes more difficult tasks that require multi-step reasoning and intricate action planning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Initial Task Pool) --> B{{Automated Task Generator}}
    B --> C[Generate Complex Tasks]
    C --> D[Apply Difficulty Enhancements]
    D --> E{Task Validity Check}
    E -- Invalid --> F[Discard Task]
    E -- Valid --> G[Verify Solvability]
    G -- Unsolvable --> F
    G -- Solvable --> H{{Expert Review/Automated QC}}
    H --> I{Filter for 'Hard' Criteria}
    I -- Fail --> F
    I -- Pass --> J[Assemble Final Dataset]
    J --> K(ACPBench Hard Dataset\nComplex Reasoning Tasks)
    K --> L[Define Evaluation Protocol]
    L --> M[Model Performance Assessment]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select 13 PDDL planning domains from ACPBench as the domain collection"] --> B["Design open-ended generative versions of 7 existing tasks plus 1 new task"]
    B["Design open-ended generative versions of 7 existing tasks plus 1 new task"] --> C["Generate 10 questions per domain for each of 8 tasks using template-based PDDL-to-NL conversion"]
    C["Generate 10 questions per domain for each of 8 tasks using template-based PDDL-to-NL conversion"] --> D["Compute plans with top-quality planner, falling back to diverse planner if needed"]
    D["Compute plans with top-quality planner, falling back to diverse planner if needed"] --> E["Store PDDL task per question with task-specific validation data for each reasoning task"]
    E["Store PDDL task per question with task-specific validation data for each reasoning task"] --> F["Develop task-specific validators to score open-ended answers based on symbolic descriptions"]
    F["Develop task-specific validators to score open-ended answers based on symbolic descriptions"] --> G["Evaluate 15 models across small, medium, large, and reasoning categories on 1040 questions"]
    G["Evaluate 15 models across small, medium, large, and reasoning categories on 1040 questions"] --> H["Apply validators to compute task-specific scores for each model's generated answers"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从ACPBench选取13个PDDL规划域作为域集合"] --> B["设计7个现有任务和1个新任务的开放式生成版本"]
    B["设计7个现有任务和1个新任务的开放式生成版本"] --> C["用模板法将PDDL转为自然语言，每域每任务生成10个问题"]
    C["用模板法将PDDL转为自然语言，每域每任务生成10个问题"] --> D["用高质量规划器计算计划，必要时回退到多样性规划器"]
    D["用高质量规划器计算计划，必要时回退到多样性规划器"] --> E["为每个问题存储PDDL任务及特定任务的验证数据"]
    E["为每个问题存储PDDL任务及特定任务的验证数据"] --> F["基于符号描述开发特定任务验证器以评分开放式答案"]
    F["基于符号描述开发特定任务验证器以评分开放式答案"] --> G["在1040个问题上评估15个小中大型及推理模型"]
    G["在1040个问题上评估15个小中大型及推理模型"] --> H["应用验证器计算每个模型生成答案的任务特定分数"]

name: ADR-Bench
intro: ADR-Bench 是一个旨在弥补中文领域评估空白的基准测试，用于评估真实世界的深度研究场景。它通过多样化的查询集和双重人工与专家评估，严格评估长周期、开放式深度研究任务。在 ADR-Bench 上，Step-DeepResearch 显著优于同类模型，并与顶级的闭源模型相媲美，证明了精细训练能够使中等规模模型以行业领先的成本效益达到专家级能力。
build_method: 混合
intro_en: ADR-Bench is a benchmark designed to fill the evaluation gap in the Chinese domain, assessing real-world deep research scenarios. It rigorously evaluates long-term, open-ended deep research tasks through a diverse query set and dual human and expert assessments. On ADR-Bench, Step-DeepResearch significantly outperforms similar models and rivals top closed-source models, demonstrating that fine-tuning enables medium-sized models to achieve expert-level capabilities with industry-leading cost efficiency.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Chinese Research Data Sources) --> B[Automated Data Collection]
    B --> C[Query Generation & Task Formulation]
    C --> D{Initial Data Filtering}
    D -- Pass --> E[Human Annotation & Curation]
    D -- Fail --> F[Discard Incomplete/Irrelevant Data]
    E --> G{{Expert Review & Validation}}
    G --> H(ADR-Bench Dataset\nDiverse Query Set)
    H --> I[Agent Model Execution]
    I --> J[Dual Human & Expert Assessment]
    J --> K[Performance Metrics Calculation]
    K --> L[Benchmark Results]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect diverse queries spanning commercial research, policy analysis, and software engineering domains"] --> B["Design open-ended, long-horizon research tasks reflecting real-world user demands in Chinese"]
    B["Design open-ended, long-horizon research tasks reflecting real-world user demands in Chinese"] --> C["Establish multi-dimensional quality criteria for evaluating research reports"]
    C["Establish multi-dimensional quality criteria for evaluating research reports"] --> D["Deploy models to generate research reports for each benchmark task"]
    D["Deploy models to generate research reports for each benchmark task"] --> E["Conduct dual human and expert evaluations across all quality dimensions"]
    E["Conduct dual human and expert evaluations across all quality dimensions"] --> F["Calculate Elo ratings for each model based on pairwise expert comparisons"]
    F["Calculate Elo ratings for each model based on pairwise expert comparisons"] --> G["Rank models by Elo scores to assess deep research capability"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["收集商业研究、政策分析和软件工程领域的多样化查询"] --> B["设计反映真实中文用户需求的开放式长周期研究任务"]
    B["设计反映真实中文用户需求的开放式长周期研究任务"] --> C["建立评估研究报告的多维度质量标准"]
    C["建立评估研究报告的多维度质量标准"] --> D["部署模型为每个基准任务生成研究报告"]
    D["部署模型为每个基准任务生成研究报告"] --> E["在所有质量维度上进行双重人工与专家评估"]
    E["在所有质量维度上进行双重人工与专家评估"] --> F["基于专家配对比较计算每个模型的Elo评分"]
    F["基于专家配对比较计算每个模型的Elo评分"] --> G["按Elo分数排名模型以评估深度研究能力"]

name: AdvancedIF
intro: AdvancedIF是一个全面的基准测试，包含1600多个提示和专家策划的评估标准，旨在评估大型语言模型遵循复杂、多轮和系统级指令的能力。它引入了RIFL（基于评估标准的指令遵循学习）训练流程，通过评估标准的生成和验证来提升模型的指令遵循能力，为更强大、更可靠的人工智能系统铺平了道路。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 生成, 对话, 指令遵循
eval_feature: 多轮, 复杂指令
intro_en: AdvancedIF is a comprehensive benchmark containing over 1,600 prompts and expert-curated evaluation criteria, designed to assess large language models' ability to follow complex, multi-turn, and system-level instructions. It introduces the RIFL (Evaluation Criteria-based Instruction Following Learning) training process, which enhances models' instruction-following capabilities through the generation and validation of evaluation criteria, paving the way for more powerful and reliable AI systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Human Experts) --> B[Instruction & Criteria Design]
    B --> C[Drafting Complex Prompts]
    B --> D[Defining Evaluation Criteria]
    C --> E[Multi-turn Instruction Formulation]
    D --> F[Expert Review & Refinement]
    E --> G[Manual Annotation]
    F --> G
    G --> H{Quality Check by Experts}
    H -->|Pass| I(Curated Prompts & Criteria)
    H -->|Fail| J[Revise & Re-annotate]
    J --> G
    I --> K(AdvancedIF Dataset\n1600+ Prompts)
    K --> L[LLM Instruction Following Assessment]
    L --> M{{RIFL Evaluation Framework}}
    M --> N(Model Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Human experts manually create prompts targeting three advanced IF categories with clear instruction-following focus"] --> B["Annotators converse with provided LLM and add instructions in the last turn"]
    B["Annotators converse with provided LLM and add instructions in the last turn"] --> C["Keep only prompts that adversarially trigger instruction-following failures in the model's final response"]
    C["Keep only prompts that adversarially trigger instruction-following failures in the model's final response"] --> D["Same annotator writes up to 20 verifiable criteria per prompt, decomposing instructions for final turn"]
    D["Same annotator writes up to 20 verifiable criteria per prompt, decomposing instructions for final turn"] --> E["Prompt o3-mini to judge whether each response satisfies each rubric criterion separately"]
    E["Prompt o3-mini to judge whether each response satisfies each rubric criterion separately"] --> F["Assign binary TRUE/False label if response satisfies all criteria for the prompt"]
    F["Assign binary TRUE/False label if response satisfies all criteria for the prompt"] --> G["Report percentage of TRUE labels for each category: ComplexIF, Carried Context, System Steerability"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["人类专家手动创建针对三类高级IF能力的提示"] --> B["标注员与LLM对话并在最后一轮添加指令"]
    B["标注员与LLM对话并在最后一轮添加指令"] --> C["仅保留对抗性触发模型最终响应失败的提示"]
    C["仅保留对抗性触发模型最终响应失败的提示"] --> D["同一标注员为每个提示编写最多20条可验证标准"]
    D["同一标注员为每个提示编写最多20条可验证标准"] --> E["提示o3-mini分别判断响应是否满足每条标准"]
    E["提示o3-mini分别判断响应是否满足每条标准"] --> F["若响应满足所有标准则分配TRUE/False二元标签"]
    F["若响应满足所有标准则分配TRUE/False二元标签"] --> G["报告每类别TRUE标签百分比：复杂IF、上下文、系统可控性"]

name: AetherCode
intro: AetherCode是一个新的基准测试，旨在通过来自IOI和ICPC等顶级编程竞赛的问题来评估大型语言模型的推理和编码能力。该基准测试通过结合具有挑战性的问题设计和稳健的评估，为LLM的能力提供了更忠实的衡量标准，并为未来代码推理领域的研究设立了新标准。
build_method: 混合
eval_feature: 专家构建
intro_en: AetherCode is a new benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' reasoning and coding abilities using problems from top programming contests like IOI and ICPC. By combining challenging problem design with robust evaluation, this benchmark offers a more faithful measure of LLM capabilities and sets a new standard for future research in code reasoning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Top Programming Contests: IOI, ICPC) --> B[Problem Extraction & Selection]
    B --> C[Problem Adaptation & Rephrasing]
    C --> D[Ground Truth Solution Generation]
    D --> E{{Automated Test Case Generation}}
    E --> F{Problem Suitability Check}
    F -->|Pass| G[Human Expert Review]
    F -->|Fail| H[Discard Unsuitable Problems]
    G --> I(AetherCode Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM Reasoning & Coding Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect problems from IOI and ICPC competitions worldwide, not online programming websites"] --> B["Categorize problems by difficulty, year, competition type/scope, format constraints, and algorithmic domains"]
    B["Categorize problems by difficulty, year, competition type/scope, format constraints, and algorithmic domains"] --> C["Exclude problems requiring visual input; label those needing special judges or custom checkers"]
    C["Exclude problems requiring visual input; label those needing special judges or custom checkers"] --> D["Generate test cases automatically using advanced methods beyond naive mutation"]
    D["Generate test cases automatically using advanced methods beyond naive mutation"] --> E["Expert competitive programmers annotate and validate test cases for correctness and coverage"]
    E["Expert competitive programmers annotate and validate test cases for correctness and coverage"] --> F["Assess test case quality by correctness and coverage criteria, not quantity alone"]
    F["Assess test case quality by correctness and coverage criteria, not quantity alone"] --> G["Evaluate models four times per problem with 32,768 token limit and report averages"]
    G["Evaluate models four times per problem with 32,768 token limit and report averages"] --> H["Calculate Pass@1, Pass@2, and Pass@4 metrics across difficulty levels and years"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从全球IOI和ICPC竞赛收集问题而非在线编程网站"] --> B["按难度、年份、竞赛类型范围、格式约束和算法领域分类"]
    B["按难度、年份、竞赛类型范围、格式约束和算法领域分类"] --> C["排除需要视觉输入的问题并标记需特殊评判器的问题"]
    C["排除需要视觉输入的问题并标记需特殊评判器的问题"] --> D["使用高级方法自动生成测试用例而非简单变异"]
    D["使用高级方法自动生成测试用例而非简单变异"] --> E["竞赛编程专家标注并验证测试用例的正确性和覆盖度"]
    E["竞赛编程专家标注并验证测试用例的正确性和覆盖度"] --> F["通过正确性和覆盖度标准评估测试用例质量而非数量"]
    F["通过正确性和覆盖度标准评估测试用例质量而非数量"] --> G["每个问题评估模型四次限制32768令牌并报告平均值"]
    G["每个问题评估模型四次限制32768令牌并报告平均值"] --> H["计算各难度级别和年份的Pass@1、Pass@2和Pass@4指标"]

name: Agent Red Teaming Benchmark
intro: Agent Red Teaming Benchmark 是一个用于评估大模型代理在真实攻击场景中安全性与鲁棒性的基准。它围绕提示注入、工具滥用和策略违背等高风险攻击进行系统化测评，用于刻画代理系统在复杂交互环境中的红队暴露面。
eval_feature: 对抗性, 过程评估
intro_en: The ART Benchmark (Agent Red Teaming) aims to evaluate the security of large language model-driven AI agents against attacks. Through a large-scale red teaming competition, researchers collected 1.8 million prompt injection attacks, over 60,000 of which successfully caused policy violations. The ART Benchmark includes these high-impact attacks and evaluates 19 advanced models, revealing critical security vulnerabilities in current AI agents.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Deploy 22 frontier AI agents across 44 realistic deployment scenarios for public competition"] --> B["Collect 1.8 million prompt-injection attacks submitted by competition participants"]
    B["Collect 1.8 million prompt-injection attacks submitted by competition participants"] --> C["Identify over 60,000 successful attacks eliciting policy violations like unauthorized access and financial misconduct"]
    C["Identify over 60,000 successful attacks eliciting policy violations like unauthorized access and financial misconduct"] --> D["Curate high-impact attacks from competition results to construct ART benchmark"]
    D["Curate high-impact attacks from competition results to construct ART benchmark"] --> E["Evaluate 19 state-of-the-art models using the curated benchmark"]
    E["Evaluate 19 state-of-the-art models using the curated benchmark"] --> F["Measure policy violations within 10-100 queries and assess attack transferability across models"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["在44个真实部署场景中部署22个前沿AI代理"] --> B["收集参赛者提交的180万次提示注入攻击"]
    B["收集参赛者提交的180万次提示注入攻击"] --> C["识别超6万次成功引发策略违规的攻击"]
    C["识别超6万次成功引发策略违规的攻击"] --> D["从竞赛结果中筛选高影响攻击构建ART基准"]
    D["从竞赛结果中筛选高影响攻击构建ART基准"] --> E["使用筛选后的基准评估19个最先进模型"]
    E["使用筛选后的基准评估19个最先进模型"] --> F["测量10-100次查询内的策略违规及跨模型迁移性"]

name: Agent-SafetyBench
intro: Agent-SafetyBench是一个综合性安全评测基准，旨在评估大型语言模型代理的安全性。该基准包含349个交互环境和2000个测试用例，覆盖8类安全风险和10种常见的失败模式。研究发现，现有主流代理的安全性堪忧，凸显了该领域巨大的提升空间。
intro_en: Agent-SafetyBench is a comprehensive safety evaluation benchmark designed to assess the safety of large language model agents. It includes 349 interactive environments and 2000 test cases, covering 8 categories of safety risks and 10 common failure modes. Research shows that the safety of current mainstream agents is concerning, highlighting significant room for improvement in this field.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Initial Safety Concepts) --> B[Scenario Ideation]
    B --> C{{Manual Annotation}}
    C --> D[Draft Test Cases]
    D --> E{{Expert Construction}}
    E --> F{Review & Refine}
    F -->|Approved| G(349 Interactive Environments)
    F -->|Approved| H(2000 Test Cases)
    F -->|Rejected| B
    G & H --> I[Categorization & Linking]
    I --> J(Agent-SafetyBench Dataset
8 Risk Categories, 10 Failure Modes)
    J --> K[LLM Agent Evaluation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Design 349 interaction environments across four tool categories with varying real-world applicability"] --> B["Create 250 test cases for each of 8 safety risk categories, totaling 2,000 cases"]
    B["Create 250 test cases for each of 8 safety risk categories, totaling 2,000 cases"] --> C["Annotate anticipated failure modes from 10 representative types for each test case"]
    C["Annotate anticipated failure modes from 10 representative types for each test case"] --> D["Conduct at least two rounds of manual review for each sample"]
    D["Conduct at least two rounds of manual review for each sample"] --> E["Apply automated validation to ensure benchmark quality"]
    E["Apply automated validation to ensure benchmark quality"] --> F["Deploy agents to dynamically interact with configurable simulated environments"]
    F["Deploy agents to dynamically interact with configurable simulated environments"] --> G["Use LLM-based scorer to automatically generate safety assessments from interaction records"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["设计349个交互环境，涵盖四类工具及不同现实应用性"] --> B["为8个安全风险类别各创建250个测试用例，共2000个"]
    B["为8个安全风险类别各创建250个测试用例，共2000个"] --> C["为每个测试用例标注10种代表性失败模式中的预期模式"]
    C["为每个测试用例标注10种代表性失败模式中的预期模式"] --> D["对每个样本进行至少两轮人工审核"]
    D["对每个样本进行至少两轮人工审核"] --> E["应用自动化验证以确保基准质量"]
    E["应用自动化验证以确保基准质量"] --> F["部署智能体与可配置模拟环境进行动态交互"]
    F["部署智能体与可配置模拟环境进行动态交互"] --> G["使用LLM评分器从交互记录自动生成安全评估"]

name: AgentDAM
intro: AgentDAM评估AI网页导航代理是否遵守数据最小化隐私原则。通过模拟真实网页场景测试代理是否仅在必要时使用敏感信息，结果显示基于GPT-4、Llama-3和Claude的代理易过度使用敏感数据。
metric: Privacy Leakage Rate, Task Success Rate
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
eval_feature: 领域特定, 对抗性
intro_en: AgentDAM evaluates whether AI web navigation agents adhere to the data minimization privacy principle. By simulating real web scenarios, it tests if agents use sensitive information only when necessary. Results show that agents based on GPT-4, Llama-3, and Claude tend to overuse sensitive data.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Web Scenarios) --> B[Simulate Web Navigation]
    B --> C{{LLM Generation: Scenarios/Prompts}}
    C --> D[Agent Interaction & Data Capture]
    D --> E(Raw Interaction Data)
    E --> F[Identify Sensitive Data Usage]
    F --> G[Manual Annotation: Privacy Adherence]
    G --> H{Annotation Quality Check}
    H -- Pass --> I(Annotated AgentDAM Dataset)
    H -- Fail --> G
    I --> J[Evaluate Agent Data Minimization]
    J --> K[Generate Evaluation Report]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
metric_en: Privacy Leakage Rate, Task Success Rate
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Categorize six types of sensitive information as potentially private data"] --> B["Construct realistic tasks for Reddit, Gitlab, and Shopping environments in WebArena simulators"]
    B["Construct realistic tasks for Reddit, Gitlab, and Shopping environments in WebArena simulators"] --> C["Embed both relevant and irrelevant private information into each task's user_data"]
    C["Embed both relevant and irrelevant private information into each task's user_data"] --> D["Agent processes user_instruction, user_data, and webpage representation to generate actions"]
    D["Agent processes user_instruction, user_data, and webpage representation to generate actions"] --> E["Execute agent actions in simulated environment, updating webpage state iteratively"]
    E["Execute agent actions in simulated environment, updating webpage state iteratively"] --> F["Evaluator judges each action for leakage of unnecessary sensitive information"]
    F["Evaluator judges each action for leakage of unnecessary sensitive information"] --> G["Measure data minimization compliance across GPT-4, Llama-3, and Claude agents"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["将六类敏感信息归类为潜在隐私数据"] --> B["为三个环境构建现实任务场景"]
    B["为三个环境构建现实任务场景"] --> C["在任务中嵌入相关和无关的隐私信息"]
    C["在任务中嵌入相关和无关的隐私信息"] --> D["代理处理指令、数据和网页生成操作"]
    D["代理处理指令、数据和网页生成操作"] --> E["在模拟环境中执行操作并更新状态"]
    E["在模拟环境中执行操作并更新状态"] --> F["评估器判断操作是否泄露非必要信息"]
    F["评估器判断操作是否泄露非必要信息"] --> G["测量各代理的数据最小化遵守情况"]

name: AgentHarm
intro: AgentHarm是用于衡量LLM代理有害性的基准，包含110个跨11类危害（如诈骗、网络犯罪）的恶意任务。评测代理在面对越狱攻击时是否服从有害请求及执行多步骤恶意行为的鲁棒性。
org: EPFL, Gray Swan AI, UK AI Safety Institute, Carnegie Mellon University, University of Oxford, Center
eval_feature: 对抗性, 多轮, 结果导向
intro_en: AgentHarm is a benchmark for measuring the harmfulness of LLM agents, containing 110 malicious tasks across 11 harm categories (e.g., scams, cybercrime). It evaluates agents' compliance with harmful requests and robustness in executing multi-step malicious behaviors under jailbreak attacks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Define Harm Categories] --> B(Harmful Scenario Concepts)
    B --> C{{Expert Brainstorming Sessions}}
    C --> D[Draft Malicious Tasks]
    D --> E[Specify Multi-step Behaviors]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -- "Pass" --> G[Refine Task Details]
    F -- "Fail" --> D
    G --> H(AgentHarm Dataset: 110 Tasks)
    H --> I[Evaluate Compliance with Harmful Requests]
    H --> J[Evaluate Robustness to Jailbreak Attacks]
    I --> K(Compliance Scores)
    J --> L(Robustness Scores)
    K & L --> M[Benchmark Results Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Manually write 110 malicious agent tasks across 11 harm categories using 104 distinct tools"] --> B["Create benign counterpart for each harmful task to measure baseline model capabilities"]
    B["Create benign counterpart for each harmful task to measure baseline model capabilities"] --> C["Augment the 110 unique tasks to produce 330 additional variants totaling 440 tasks"]
    C["Augment the 110 unique tasks to produce 330 additional variants totaling 440 tasks"] --> D["Have separate human reviewers manually review all written tasks for quality"]
    D["Have separate human reviewers manually review all written tasks for quality"] --> E["Withhold 30% of tasks from public LLM APIs to prevent contamination"]
    E["Withhold 30% of tasks from public LLM APIs to prevent contamination"] --> F["Write human rubrics specifying narrow subtasks for LLM judges to evaluate agent outputs"]
    F["Write human rubrics specifying narrow subtasks for LLM judges to evaluate agent outputs"] --> G["Integrate proxy tools into evaluation framework for multi-step agent execution"]
    G["Integrate proxy tools into evaluation framework for multi-step agent execution"] --> H["Evaluate LLMs with and without jailbreaks on harmful and benign tasks measuring compliance and capability"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["人工编写110个恶意代理任务，覆盖11类危害和104种工具"] --> B["为每个有害任务创建良性对应版本以测量基线能力"]
    B["为每个有害任务创建良性对应版本以测量基线能力"] --> C["将110个独特任务增强为330个变体，共440个任务"]
    C["将110个独特任务增强为330个变体，共440个任务"] --> D["由独立人工审核员审查所有编写的任务"]
    D["由独立人工审核员审查所有编写的任务"] --> E["保留30%任务不公开以防污染"]
    E["保留30%任务不公开以防污染"] --> F["编写人工评分标准，指定LLM评判的细分子任务"]
    F["编写人工评分标准，指定LLM评判的细分子任务"] --> G["将代理工具集成到评估框架以执行多步骤任务"]
    G["将代理工具集成到评估框架以执行多步骤任务"] --> H["在有害和良性任务上评估LLM，测量服从性和能力保持"]

name: AGIEval
intro: AGIEval是一个以人为中心的基准测试，旨在评估基础模型在人类水平任务上的通用能力。它通过使用标准化考试（如高考、法学院入学考试和数学竞赛）来评估模型，以反映模型在现实世界场景中的表现。该基准测试揭示了模型的优势和局限性，为提升其通用能力提供了有价值的见解。
build_method: 考试题改编
eval_feature: 思维链, 零样本
intro_en: AGIEval is a human-centered benchmark designed to evaluate the general capabilities of foundation models on human-level tasks. It assesses models using standardized exams such as the Gaokao, law school entrance exams, and math competitions to reflect their performance in real-world scenarios. This benchmark reveals the strengths and limitations of models, providing valuable insights for improving their general abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Standardized Exams) --> B[Exam Acquisition]
    B --> C[Question Extraction & Digitization]
    C --> D[Question Adaptation for AI]
    D --> E[Formatting & Standardization]
    E --> F{Human Expert Review}
    F -->|Pass| G(AGIEval Benchmark Dataset)
    F -->|Fail| H[Refine/Discard]
    G --> I[Foundation Model Assessment]
    I --> J(Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect questions from official standardized exams including Gaokao, SAT, LSAT, math competitions, and civil service tests"] --> B["Curate 20 language-only tasks covering Chinese and English across diverse subjects and exam types"]
    B["Curate 20 language-only tasks covering Chinese and English across diverse subjects and exam types"] --> C["Obtain human performance baselines including average scores and top performer scores from official exam statistics"]
    C["Obtain human performance baselines including average scores and top performer scores from official exam statistics"] --> D["Evaluate foundation models using zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting settings via public APIs"]
    D["Evaluate foundation models using zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting settings via public APIs"] --> E["Compare model accuracy against average and top human performance across all 20 tasks"]
    E["Compare model accuracy against average and top human performance across all 20 tasks"] --> F["Analyze model capabilities across four dimensions: understanding, knowledge, reasoning, and calculation"]
    F["Analyze model capabilities across four dimensions: understanding, knowledge, reasoning, and calculation"] --> G["Release all data, code, and model outputs publicly for reproducibility and further research"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从高考、SAT、法考等官方标准化考试中收集题目"] --> B["整理20个中英双语任务涵盖多学科和考试类型"]
    B["整理20个中英双语任务涵盖多学科和考试类型"] --> C["从官方统计获取人类平均分和顶尖表现基线"]
    C["从官方统计获取人类平均分和顶尖表现基线"] --> D["用零样本、少样本和思维链提示评估基础模型"]
    D["用零样本、少样本和思维链提示评估基础模型"] --> E["对比模型准确率与人类平均和顶尖表现"]
    E["对比模型准确率与人类平均和顶尖表现"] --> F["分析模型在理解、知识、推理和计算四个维度的能力"]
    F["分析模型在理解、知识、推理和计算四个维度的能力"] --> G["公开发布所有数据、代码和模型输出供研究使用"]

name: Aider Polyglot
intro: Aider Polyglot 基准测试评估大型语言模型在多语言代码编辑方面的能力。它包含225个来自Exercism平台的编程练习，涵盖C++、Go、Java、JavaScript、Python和Rust。该基准测试专注于最具挑战性的问题，旨在区分顶级代码生成模型的性能。
intro_en: The Aider Polyglot benchmark evaluates large language models' abilities in multilingual code editing. It includes 225 programming exercises from the Exercism platform, covering C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust. This benchmark focuses on the most challenging problems to differentiate the performance of top code generation models.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Exercism Platform) --> B[Select Programming Exercises]
    B --> C{Filter by Difficulty}
    C -- "Most Challenging" --> D(Raw Programming Exercises)
    D --> E[Standardize Problem Format]
    E --> F[Define Multilingual Code Editing Tasks]
    F --> G{{Hybrid Data Curation}}
    G --> H(Aider Polyglot Dataset\n225 Exercises: C++, Go, Java, JS, Python, Rust)
    H --> I[Evaluate LLM Code Editing Ability]
    I --> J[Benchmark Results]

name: AIME
intro: MathArena是一个创新的数学基准测试，它利用如AIME等定期举办的数学竞赛中的高质量、高难度问题，对大型语言模型进行实时评估。该基准通过在题目发布后立即进行评估，有效避免了数据污染问题。MathArena不仅评估模型的解题准确性，还是首个评估模型证明撰写能力的基准。
task_type: 数学问题, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 领域特定, 防污染
intro_en: MathArena is an innovative math benchmark that uses high-quality, challenging problems from regularly held math competitions like AIME to evaluate large language models in real time. By assessing models immediately after problem release, it effectively prevents data contamination. MathArena not only measures solution accuracy but is also the first benchmark to evaluate models' proof-writing abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Math Competitions: AIME) --> B[Problem Acquisition]
    B --> C{{Expert Selection Method}}
    C --> D[Problem Curation]
    D --> E{Problem Quality Check}
    E -->|Pass| F[Expert Verification]
    E -->|Fail| G[Problem Rejection]
    F --> H(High-Quality Problems)
    H --> I(MathArena Dataset)
    I --> J[Real-time LLM Evaluation]
    J --> K(LLM Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select sufficiently challenging and reputable recurring math competition"] --> B["Extract problems and ground-truth solutions from competition materials"]
    B["Extract problems and ground-truth solutions from competition materials"] --> C["Query selected models immediately after competition release to prevent contamination"]
    C["Query selected models immediately after competition release to prevent contamination"] --> D["Parse final-answer problems using automated rule-based parser to extract answers"]
    D["Parse final-answer problems using automated rule-based parser to extract answers"] --> E["Evaluate proof-based problems using human graders to assess reasoning correctness"]
    E["Evaluate proof-based problems using human graders to assess reasoning correctness"] --> F["Compute leaderboard rankings with statistical post-processing for accuracy"]
    F["Compute leaderboard rankings with statistical post-processing for accuracy"] --> G["Publish results on public leaderboard with GUI for viewing model responses"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["选择足够有挑战性且信誉良好的定期数学竞赛"] --> B["从竞赛材料中提取问题和标准答案"]
    B["从竞赛材料中提取问题和标准答案"] --> C["竞赛发布后立即查询选定模型以防止污染"]
    C["竞赛发布后立即查询选定模型以防止污染"] --> D["使用自动化规则解析器提取最终答案类问题"]
    D["使用自动化规则解析器提取最终答案类问题"] --> E["使用人工评分员评估证明类问题的推理正确性"]
    E["使用人工评分员评估证明类问题的推理正确性"] --> F["通过统计后处理计算排行榜排名以确保准确性"]
    F["通过统计后处理计算排行榜排名以确保准确性"] --> G["在公开排行榜发布结果并提供界面查看模型回答"]

name: AInsteinBench
intro: AInsteinBench是一个用于评估大型语言模型在科学计算开发中能力的基准测试。它基于真实的科研软件生态系统，任务来源于六个科学领域的代码拉取请求，涵盖量子化学和分子动力学等。该基准旨在考察模型完成复杂的、由测试驱动的端到端编码任务的能力，而不仅仅是简单的代码生成。
intro_en: AInsteinBench is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' capabilities in scientific computing development. It is based on real scientific software ecosystems, with tasks sourced from code pull requests across six scientific fields, including quantum chemistry and molecular dynamics. The benchmark aims to assess models' ability to complete complex, test-driven end-to-end coding tasks, rather than simple code generation.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Scientific Software Ecosystems) --> B[Web Crawling]
    B --> C(Raw Code Pull Requests)
    C --> D[Extract Scientific Computing Tasks]
    D --> E{Categorize by 6 Scientific Fields}
    E --> F[Standardize Task Format]
    F --> G{{Expert Review & Curation}}
    G --> H{Task Validity Check}
    H -- Fail --> I[Discard/Refine]
    H -- Pass --> J(AInsteinBench Final Dataset)
    J --> K[Evaluate LLMs]
    K --> L(Scientific Computing Capabilities)
    L --> M[Performance Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect issue-linked pull requests from six scientific repositories across quantum chemistry, computing, and dynamics domains"] --> B["Extract before and after code states, environment specifications, and domain-specific metadata from each pull request"]
    B["Extract before and after code states, environment specifications, and domain-specific metadata from each pull request"] --> C["Apply multi-stage filtering to ensure scientific challenge, adequate test coverage, and well-calibrated difficulty"]
    C["Apply multi-stage filtering to ensure scientific challenge, adequate test coverage, and well-calibrated difficulty"] --> D["Conduct expert review to validate scientific meaningfulness and reproducibility standards of filtered tasks"]
    D["Conduct expert review to validate scientific meaningfulness and reproducibility standards of filtered tasks"] --> E["Configure executable environments for each task with production-grade scientific repository dependencies and HPC settings"]
    E["Configure executable environments for each task with production-grade scientific repository dependencies and HPC settings"] --> F["Evaluate model-generated patches using test-driven verification in executable environments against original test suites"]
    F["Evaluate model-generated patches using test-driven verification in executable environments against original test suites"] --> G["Measure performance by test pass rates and analyze scientifically meaningful failure modes across difficulty tiers"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从六个科学代码库收集关联问题的拉取请求"] --> B["提取每个请求的前后代码状态和环境规范"]
    B["提取每个请求的前后代码状态和环境规范"] --> C["多阶段过滤确保科学挑战性和测试覆盖度"]
    C["多阶段过滤确保科学挑战性和测试覆盖度"] --> D["专家审查验证任务的科学意义和可重现性"]
    D["专家审查验证任务的科学意义和可重现性"] --> E["为每个任务配置可执行环境和依赖项"]
    E["为每个任务配置可执行环境和依赖项"] --> F["在可执行环境中用测试驱动验证模型补丁"]
    F["在可执行环境中用测试驱动验证模型补丁"] --> G["通过测试通过率衡量性能并分析失败模式"]

name: AIRS-Bench
intro: AIRS-Bench 是一个为前沿人工智能科研 Agent 设计的基准测试套件，包含20个源自顶尖机器学习论文的任务。它旨在评估 Agent 在整个研究生命周期中的能力，涵盖语言建模、数学和生物信息学等多个领域，并促进自主科学研究的发展。该基准的设计灵活，方便集成新任务和进行跨框架的严格比较。
intro_en: AIRS-Bench is a benchmark suite designed for cutting-edge AI research Agents, comprising 20 tasks sourced from top machine learning papers. It aims to evaluate Agents' capabilities across the entire research lifecycle, covering areas such as language modeling, mathematics, and bioinformatics, and promotes autonomous scientific research. The benchmark is flexibly designed for easy integration of new tasks and rigorous cross-framework comparisons.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Top Machine Learning Papers) --> B[Task Identification]
    B --> C{Relevance & Scope Filter}
    C -- Pass --> D[Expert Task Design]
    C -- Fail --> E[Discard Irrelevant Tasks]
    D --> F[Task Specification & Formatting]
    F --> G(Initial Task Definitions)
    G --> H[Expert Review & Validation]
    H --> I{Quality & Completeness Check}
    I -- Approved --> J(AIRS-Bench Task Suite)
    I -- Revise --> D
    J --> K[Agent Capability Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
    L --> M(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Source candidate tasks from PapersWithCode leaderboards with SOTA results published between 2020 and 2025"] --> B["Filter datasets for availability via HuggingFace and existence of train and test splits"]
    B["Filter datasets for availability via HuggingFace and existence of train and test splits"] --> C["Manually create each task and verify SOTA result by confirming the metric in the cited paper"]
    C["Manually create each task and verify SOTA result by confirming the metric in the cited paper"] --> D["Generate approximately 100 tasks from 85 different machine learning papers and datasets"]
    D["Generate approximately 100 tasks from 85 different machine learning papers and datasets"] --> E["Select 20 tasks spanning 7 categories to ensure benchmark tractability and accuracy"]
    E["Select 20 tasks spanning 7 categories to ensure benchmark tractability and accuracy"] --> F["Define task fields including problem description, dataset identifier, metric, and SOTA score in metadata"]
    F["Define task fields including problem description, dataset identifier, metric, and SOTA score in metadata"] --> G["Agent develops solution generating predictions on test labels without baseline code provided"]
    G["Agent develops solution generating predictions on test labels without baseline code provided"] --> H["Evaluate agent predictions against SOTA result using specified metric to determine if benchmark is beaten"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从PapersWithCode排行榜获取2020至2025年间发表的SOTA结果任务"] --> B["筛选HuggingFace可用且具有训练测试划分的数据集"]
    B["筛选HuggingFace可用且具有训练测试划分的数据集"] --> C["手动创建每个任务并通过引用论文确认SOTA结果的指标"]
    C["手动创建每个任务并通过引用论文确认SOTA结果的指标"] --> D["从85篇不同机器学习论文和数据集生成约100个任务"]
    D["从85篇不同机器学习论文和数据集生成约100个任务"] --> E["选择跨7个类别的20个任务以确保基准的可操作性和准确性"]
    E["选择跨7个类别的20个任务以确保基准的可操作性和准确性"] --> F["在元数据中定义任务字段包括问题描述数据集标识符指标和SOTA分数"]
    F["在元数据中定义任务字段包括问题描述数据集标识符指标和SOTA分数"] --> G["智能体在无基线代码情况下开发方案生成测试标签预测"]
    G["智能体在无基线代码情况下开发方案生成测试标签预测"] --> H["使用指定指标评估智能体预测与SOTA结果对比判断是否超越基准"]

name: ALERT
intro: ALERT是一个与模型无关的推荐解释评估基准。它包含一个涵盖15个亚马逊电商类别、包含2761个用户交互的的多样化数据集。该基准还引入了两种新颖的基于大型语言模型（LLM）的自动评估器，能够实现可扩展且与人类偏好对齐的解释评估，旨在推动更有效的推荐解释系统的发展。
org: Worcester Polytechnic Institute, Amazon.com, Northwestern University, University of Notre Dame, Univ
eval_feature: 领域特定, LLM-as-Judge
intro_en: ALERT is a model-agnostic recommendation explanation evaluation benchmark. It includes a diverse dataset of 2,761 user interactions across 15 Amazon e-commerce categories. The benchmark also introduces two novel LLM-based automatic evaluators that enable scalable, human-aligned explanation assessment, aiming to advance more effective recommendation explanation systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Amazon E-commerce Data) --> B[Extract User Interactions]
    B --> C(Raw User Interactions)
    C --> D[Categorize & Structure Data]
    D --> E{{LLM Generation}}
    E --> F(LLM Generated Explanations)
    F --> G[Manual Review]
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(Curated Data & Explanations)
    H -->|Fail| J[Discard/Revise]
    I --> K(ALERT Benchmark Dataset\n2,761 Interactions, 15 Categories)
    K --> L{{LLM-based Automatic Evaluators}}
    L --> M[Model Evaluation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Sample user-item interactions from Amazon across 15 categories with purchase history data"] --> B["Filter items by title length and users by transaction count; retain only 5-star purchases"]
    B["Filter items by title length and users by transaction count; retain only 5-star purchases"] --> C["Cap at 200 purchase behaviors per category to balance diversity and prevent category bias"]
    C["Cap at 200 purchase behaviors per category to balance diversity and prevent category bias"] --> D["Develop evaluation criteria collaboratively with e-commerce editorial team and refine through user studies"]
    D["Develop evaluation criteria collaboratively with e-commerce editorial team and refine through user studies"] --> E["Deploy ALERT-Gen and ALERT-Disc LLM evaluators to judge explanation quality using the criteria"]
    E["Deploy ALERT-Gen and ALERT-Disc LLM evaluators to judge explanation quality using the criteria"] --> F["Apply divide-and-aggregate approach to synthesize multiple LLM judgments under each criterion"]
    F["Apply divide-and-aggregate approach to synthesize multiple LLM judgments under each criterion"] --> G["Create meta-evaluation dataset with intentional negative examples and expert human annotations"]
    G["Create meta-evaluation dataset with intentional negative examples and expert human annotations"] --> H["Validate evaluator reliability by measuring concordance with expert human judgments"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从亚马逊15个类别中采样用户-物品交互及购买历史"] --> B["按标题长度和交易数过滤并仅保留五星购买"]
    B["按标题长度和交易数过滤并仅保留五星购买"] --> C["每类限200条购买行为以平衡多样性和防偏"]
    C["每类限200条购买行为以平衡多样性和防偏"] --> D["与电商编辑团队协作制定评估标准并通过用户研究优化"]
    D["与电商编辑团队协作制定评估标准并通过用户研究优化"] --> E["部署ALERT-Gen和ALERT-Disc评估器依标准判断解释质量"]
    E["部署ALERT-Gen和ALERT-Disc评估器依标准判断解释质量"] --> F["应用分治聚合法综合各标准下的多个LLM判断"]
    F["应用分治聚合法综合各标准下的多个LLM判断"] --> G["创建含故意负例和专家标注的元评估数据集"]
    G["创建含故意负例和专家标注的元评估数据集"] --> H["通过与专家判断的一致性验证评估器可靠性"]

name: AlignBench
intro: AlignBench是一个多维度中文大语言模型对齐评测基准。它采用人机协同的数据构建流程，包含683个源于真实场景的查询，并为知识密集型问题提供了可靠的网络证据。该基准利用结合思维链的规则校准多维LLM评审机制进行自动化评估，以确保结果的高可靠性和可解释性。
task_type: 开放式问答, 生成, 对话
eval_feature: 思维链, 多轮
intro_en: AlignBench is a multidimensional Chinese large language model alignment benchmark. It uses a human-AI collaborative data construction process, containing 683 queries derived from real-world scenarios, and provides reliable web evidence for knowledge-intensive questions. The benchmark employs an automated evaluation mechanism combining chain-of-thought reasoning with rule-based calibration for multidimensional LLM review, ensuring high reliability and interpretability of results.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Human-AI Collaborative Data Construction]
    B --> C[Derive 683 Queries]
    C --> D(Initial Query Set)
    D --> E[Integrate Web Evidence]
    E --> F{Knowledge-intensive Question Validation}
    F -->|Pass| G[Refine & Augment Queries]
    F -->|Fail| H[Discard/Re-evaluate]
    G --> I(Final AlignBench Dataset\n683 Queries)
    I --> J[LLM Alignment Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Curate 683 queries from real user scenarios across eight main categories"] --> B["Annotators collect web evidences with URLs and quotations for knowledge-intensive queries"]
    B["Annotators collect web evidences with URLs and quotations for knowledge-intensive queries"] --> C["Pre-generate reference answers for the curated queries"]
    C["Pre-generate reference answers for the curated queries"] --> D["Human annotators verify and improve the pre-generated references"]
    D["Human annotators verify and improve the pre-generated references"] --> E["Use LLMs as difficulty filters and classify queries on the taxonomy"]
    E["Use LLMs as difficulty filters and classify queries on the taxonomy"] --> F["Generate LLM responses to the benchmark queries for evaluation"]
    F["Generate LLM responses to the benchmark queries for evaluation"] --> G["Apply rule-calibrated LLM-as-Judge with Chain-of-Thought for multi-dimensional point-wise grading"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从真实用户场景策划683个查询，涵盖八大类别"] --> B["标注员为知识密集型问题收集网络证据及引用"]
    B["标注员为知识密集型问题收集网络证据及引用"] --> C["为策划的查询预生成参考答案"]
    C["为策划的查询预生成参考答案"] --> D["人工标注员验证并改进预生成的参考答案"]
    D["人工标注员验证并改进预生成的参考答案"] --> E["使用LLM作为难度过滤器并对查询分类"]
    E["使用LLM作为难度过滤器并对查询分类"] --> F["生成待评测LLM对基准查询的回复"]
    F["生成待评测LLM对基准查询的回复"] --> G["应用规则校准的LLM评审结合思维链进行多维度评分"]

name: All-Angles
intro: All-Angles Bench是一个旨在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）多视角理解能力的基准测试。它包含2100多个经过人工精心标注的多视角问答对，涵盖90个不同的真实世界场景。该基准测试通过六项任务（计数、属性识别、相对距离、相对方向、物体操作和相机姿态估计）来评估模型在多视角几何一致性和跨视角对应方面的能力。实验结果表明，当前MLLMs在处理部分遮挡视图的跨视角对应和建立粗略相机姿态方面表现不佳，与人类水平存在显著差距。
org: University of California, Berkeley, Meta, New York University, Kennesaw State University, National T
build_method: 人工标注
eval_feature: 多视角, 几何一致性, 跨视角对应
task_type_en: Multiple Choice, Open-ended QA, Visual QA
intro_en: All-Angles Bench is a benchmark designed to evaluate the multi-view understanding capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). It contains over 2,100 carefully annotated multi-view question-answer pairs across 90 different real-world scenarios. The benchmark assesses models through six tasks—counting, attribute recognition, relative distance, relative direction, object manipulation, and camera pose estimation—to evaluate multi-view geometric consistency and cross-view correspondence. Experimental results show that current MLLMs perform poorly in cross-view correspondence with partially occluded views and in estimating coarse camera poses, with a significant gap compared to human-level performance.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Identify 90 Scenarios]
    B --> C(Selected Scenarios Data)
    C --> D[Gather Multi-view Data]
    D --> E(Raw Multi-view Data)
    E --> F{{Manual Annotation}}
    F --> G[Annotate Q&A Pairs]
    G --> H(Annotated Draft Data)
    H --> I[Perform Quality Control]
    I --> J{Annotation Quality Check}
    J -->|Pass| K(Final Dataset: 2100+ Q&A Pairs)
    J -->|Fail| L[Re-annotation/Refinement]
    L --> G
    K --> M[Evaluate MLLMs]
    M --> N(Six Assessment Types)
    N --> O(Benchmark Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select 90 multi-view scenes from Ego-Exo4D and EgoHumans with at least three viewpoints per scene"] --> B["Design six task categories targeting multi-view reasoning: counting, attributes, distance, direction, manipulation, and camera pose"]
    B["Design six task categories targeting multi-view reasoning: counting, attributes, distance, direction, manipulation, and camera pose"] --> C["Use an MLLM to generate initial questions from multi-view data, three per category except one for camera pose"]
    C["Use an MLLM to generate initial questions from multi-view data, three per category except one for camera pose"] --> D["Eight human annotators review questions with images, remove invalid entries, and refine question phrasing"]
    D["Eight human annotators review questions with images, remove invalid entries, and refine question phrasing"] --> E["Annotators revise incorrect answer choices and label the single correct answer for each multiple-choice question"]
    E["Annotators revise incorrect answer choices and label the single correct answer for each multiple-choice question"] --> F["Create paired questions by rephrasing or altering view order while preserving underlying visual correspondences"]
    F["Create paired questions by rephrasing or altering view order while preserving underlying visual correspondences"] --> G["Benchmark 27 MLLMs against human evaluators on a 250-question subset across all six task categories"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从Ego-Exo4D和EgoHumans选取90个多视角场景"] --> B["设计六类任务评估多视角推理能力"]
    B["设计六类任务评估多视角推理能力"] --> C["用MLLM从多视角数据生成初始问题"]
    C["用MLLM从多视角数据生成初始问题"] --> D["八名标注员审查问题并优化表述"]
    D["八名标注员审查问题并优化表述"] --> E["标注员修正错误选项并标注正确答案"]
    E["标注员修正错误选项并标注正确答案"] --> F["创建配对问题以改变措辞或视角顺序"]
    F["创建配对问题以改变措辞或视角顺序"] --> G["在250题子集上对比27个模型与人类表现"]

name: AlpacaEval 2.0
intro: AlpacaEval 2.0是一个自动化评估框架，利用基于GPT-4的成对比较生成模型胜率。引入长度控制胜率，减少模型偏好长回答的偏差，提高与人类偏好的相关性和评测鲁棒性。
task_type: 排序, 对话, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 结果导向, 思维链
intro_en: AlpacaEval 2.0 is an automated evaluation framework that uses GPT-4-based pairwise comparisons to generate model win rates. It introduces length-controlled win rates to reduce bias toward longer responses, enhancing correlation with human preferences and evaluation robustness.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect 805 instructions representative of Alpaca web demo user interactions"] --> B["Generate responses from baseline model GPT-4 turbo for all instructions"]
    B["Generate responses from baseline model GPT-4 turbo for all instructions"] --> C["Generate responses from evaluated model for all instructions"]
    C["Generate responses from evaluated model for all instructions"] --> D["Use GPT-4 turbo evaluator to compare response pairs and output preference probabilities"]
    D["Use GPT-4 turbo evaluator to compare response pairs and output preference probabilities"] --> E["Compute standard win rate as expected preference probability across 805 instructions"]
    E["Compute standard win rate as expected preference probability across 805 instructions"] --> F["Fit generalized linear model predicting preferences from model identity, length difference, and features"]
    F["Fit generalized linear model predicting preferences from model identity, length difference, and features"] --> G["Compute length-controlled win rate by controlling for length difference in regression model"]
    G["Compute length-controlled win rate by controlling for length difference in regression model"] --> H["Validate length-controlled metric correlation with LMSYS Chatbot Arena human evaluations"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["收集805条代表Alpaca演示用户交互的指令"] --> B["用基线模型GPT-4 turbo生成所有指令的回复"]
    B["用基线模型GPT-4 turbo生成所有指令的回复"] --> C["用待评估模型生成所有指令的回复"]
    C["用待评估模型生成所有指令的回复"] --> D["用GPT-4 turbo评估器比较回复对并输出偏好概率"]
    D["用GPT-4 turbo评估器比较回复对并输出偏好概率"] --> E["计算标准胜率为805条指令的期望偏好概率"]
    E["计算标准胜率为805条指令的期望偏好概率"] --> F["拟合广义线性模型预测偏好基于模型身份长度差异等特征"]
    F["拟合广义线性模型预测偏好基于模型身份长度差异等特征"] --> G["通过回归模型控制长度差异计算长度控制胜率"]
    G["通过回归模型控制长度差异计算长度控制胜率"] --> H["验证长度控制指标与LMSYS竞技场人类评估的相关性"]

name: AMEGA-LLM
intro: AMEGA-LLM是评估大型语言模型对医疗指南依从性的基准，通过模拟临床交互和开放式问答，涵盖24个诊断场景和13个医学专业，提供比传统基准更全面真实的评估。
org: DATEXIS, Beuth University of Applied Sciences Berlin
scale: 24个诊断场景，163个问题，1497个评估标准
build_method_en: Manual Annotation, Medical Guideline Experts Construction
eval_feature_en: Evaluate LLM compliance with medical guidelines, Simulate clinical interaction, Open-ended QA format
intro_en: AMEGA-LLM is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' adherence to medical guidelines. It offers a more comprehensive and realistic assessment than traditional benchmarks by simulating clinical interactions and open-ended Q&A across 24 diagnostic scenarios and 13 medical specialties.

    J --> L[Model Evaluation: Entity Generation]
    K --> M(Evaluation Metrics)
    L --> M

flowchart LR
    A["Experienced physicians craft 20 clinical cases across 13 specialties adhering to current medical guidelines"] --> B["Design 135 open-ended questions with 1337 weighted scoring criteria mirroring real clinical interactions"]
    B["Design 135 open-ended questions with 1337 weighted scoring criteria mirroring real clinical interactions"] --> C["Candidate LLM receives initial case and generates responses to questions across multiple sections"]

    C["候选LLM接收初始病例并对多个部分的问题生成回答"] --> D["GPT-4评估模型根据预定义标准评估候选模型对各问题部分的回答"]
    D["GPT-4评估模型根据预定义标准评估候选模型对各问题部分的回答"] --> E["若初始回答未达标准则重问流程允许候选模型优化答案"]
    E["若初始回答未达标准则重问流程允许候选模型优化答案"] --> F["通过乘法和加法聚合标准分数计算问题、部分和病例级得分"]
    F["通过乘法和加法聚合标准分数计算问题、部分和病例级得分"] --> G["验证GPT-4评估器与人类专家在主要诊断上的一致性达0.98 F1分数"]
    G["验证GPT-4评估器与人类专家在主要诊断上的一致性达0.98 F1分数"] --> H["分析各问题类型、病例和医学专业的归一化分数及性能缺陷"]

name: AndroidWorld
intro: AndroidWorld是一个功能齐全的Android环境，为20个真实Android应用中的116个程序化任务提供奖励信号。与现有交互式环境不同，AndroidWorld动态构建参数化任务，并以自然语言表达，从而实现更大、更真实的任务测试。它通过专用初始化、成功检查和拆卸逻辑确保可复现性，并支持对设备系统状态的修改和检查。
build_method: 自动生成, 专家构建
intro_en: AndroidWorld is a fully functional Android environment that provides reward signals for 116 programmatic tasks across 20 real Android apps. Unlike existing interactive environments, AndroidWorld dynamically constructs parameterized tasks expressed in natural language, enabling larger and more realistic task evaluations. It ensures reproducibility through dedicated initialization, success checks, and teardown logic, and supports modification and inspection of device system states.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Android Apps) --> B[Task Parameterization]
    B --> C{{Automatic Generation}}
    C --> D[Dynamic Task Construction]
    D --> E{{Expert Construction}}
    E --> F(116 Programmatic Tasks)
    F --> G(AndroidWorld Environment)
    G --> H[Agent Interaction]
    H --> I(Reward Signals)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select 116 task templates across 20 real-world Android apps for practical everyday activities"] --> B["Implement parameterized task generation with random parameters for unlimited task instances"]
    B["Implement parameterized task generation with random parameters for unlimited task instances"] --> C["Write initialization, success-checking, and tear-down logic for each task to ensure reproducibility"]
    C["Write initialization, success-checking, and tear-down logic for each task to ensure reproducibility"] --> D["Annotators perform tasks, assign difficulty levels, estimate step counts, and select category tags"]
    D["Annotators perform tasks, assign difficulty levels, estimate step counts, and select category tags"] --> E["Configure Android emulator with UI tree and function-calling APIs as observation and action space"]
    E["Configure Android emulator with UI tree and function-calling APIs as observation and action space"] --> F["Test baseline agents on dynamically generated tasks and measure task completion rates"]
    F["Test baseline agents on dynamically generated tasks and measure task completion rates"] --> G["Conduct robustness analysis by testing agent performance across task parameter variations"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从20个真实Android应用中选择116个日常任务模板"] --> B["实现参数化任务生成以创建无限任务实例"]
    B["实现参数化任务生成以创建无限任务实例"] --> C["为每个任务编写初始化、成功检查和拆卸逻辑"]
    C["为每个任务编写初始化、成功检查和拆卸逻辑"] --> D["标注员执行任务并分配难度、估计步数、选择类别标签"]
    D["标注员执行任务并分配难度、估计步数、选择类别标签"] --> E["配置Android模拟器的UI树和API作为观察和动作空间"]
    E["配置Android模拟器的UI树和API作为观察和动作空间"] --> F["在动态生成的任务上测试基线智能体并测量完成率"]
    F["在动态生成的任务上测试基线智能体并测量完成率"] --> G["通过任务参数变化测试智能体性能进行鲁棒性分析"]

name: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge)
intro: ARC（AI2推理挑战赛）是一个由艾伦人工智能研究所（AI2）发布的科学问答数据集。它包含7787个从小学到高中的非图表类科学问题，旨在评估AI系统在需要深度推理的复杂问题上的表现。该基准分为“简单”和“挑战”两个部分，其中“挑战”部分的问题是现有算法难以正确解答的。
build_method: 专家构建, 考试题
intro_en: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge) is a science question-answering dataset released by the Allen Institute for AI (AI2). It contains 7,787 non-diagram science questions ranging from elementary to high school level, designed to evaluate AI systems' performance on complex problems requiring deep reasoning. The benchmark is divided into "Easy" and "Challenge" sections, with the "Challenge" section containing questions that existing algorithms struggle to answer correctly.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Educational Materials) --> B[Expert Selection & Curation]
    B --> C{Filter: Non-Diagram Questions}
    C -->|Pass| D[Question Formatting & Structuring]
    C -->|Fail| J[Discard Irrelevant Questions]
    D --> E{{Expert Review for Reasoning Complexity}}
    E --> F(ARC Dataset: 7,787 Science Questions)
    F --> G[AI Model Inference]
    G --> H[Performance Evaluation Metrics]
    H --> I(AI System Performance Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect 7,787 natural science questions authored for standardized tests from grade-school level"] --> B["Apply retrieval-based algorithm to answer all questions and record results"]
    B["Apply retrieval-based algorithm to answer all questions and record results"] --> C["Apply word co-occurrence algorithm to answer all questions and record results"]
    C["Apply word co-occurrence algorithm to answer all questions and record results"] --> D["Partition questions into Challenge Set where both algorithms failed and Easy Set for remainder"]
    D["Partition questions into Challenge Set where both algorithms failed and Easy Set for remainder"] --> E["Assemble ARC Corpus containing 14 million science sentences relevant to the questions"]
    E["Assemble ARC Corpus containing 14 million science sentences relevant to the questions"] --> F["Test neural baseline models from SQuAD and SNLI on the Challenge Set"]
    F["Test neural baseline models from SQuAD and SNLI on the Challenge Set"] --> G["Compare baseline model performance against random baseline to validate difficulty"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["收集7787道标准化考试的自然科学题"] --> B["用检索算法回答所有问题并记录结果"]
    B["用检索算法回答所有问题并记录结果"] --> C["用词共现算法回答所有问题并记录结果"]
    C["用词共现算法回答所有问题并记录结果"] --> D["将两算法均失败的题划入挑战集其余划入简单集"]
    D["将两算法均失败的题划入挑战集其余划入简单集"] --> E["构建包含1400万科学句子的语料库"]
    E["构建包含1400万科学句子的语料库"] --> F["用SQuAD和SNLI神经模型测试挑战集"]
    F["用SQuAD和SNLI神经模型测试挑战集"] --> G["对比基线模型与随机基线验证难度"]

name: ARC-AGI
intro: ARC-AGI基准测试系列旨在衡量在新型任务上的少样本泛化能力，这是智能的核心方面。ARC Prize 2025全球竞赛使用了ARC-AGI-2数据集，其任务复杂性更高。该基准测试已成为AI推理的行业标准，但目前的AI推理性能仍受限于知识覆盖范围。ARC-AGI-3引入了需要探索、规划、记忆、目标获取和对齐能力的交互式推理挑战。
build_method: 专家构建, 自动生成
task_type: 抽象推理, 交互式任务
eval_feature: 少样本, 过程评估
intro_en: The ARC-AGI benchmark series aims to measure few-shot generalization ability on novel tasks, a core aspect of intelligence. The ARC Prize 2025 global competition uses the ARC-AGI-2 dataset, which features higher task complexity. This benchmark has become the industry standard for AI reasoning, but current AI reasoning performance remains limited by knowledge coverage. ARC-AGI-3 introduces interactive reasoning challenges requiring exploration, planning, memory, goal acquisition, and alignment capabilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Human Expert Knowledge) --> C[Expert Task Design]
    B(Task Specification Rules) --> D[Automated Task Generation]
    C --> E{Complexity Filtering}
    D --> E
    E --> F{Novelty Validation}
    F --> G[Expert Review]
    G --> H[Difficulty Assessment]
    H --> I(ARC-AGI-2 Dataset\nHigh Complexity, Novel Tasks)
    I --> J[Few-shot Generalization Evaluation]
    J --> K[AI Model Reasoning Test]
    K --> L(Benchmark Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Create tasks by humans with unique logic requiring independent rule discovery"] --> B["Design tasks with greater complexity and generalization difficulty than ARC-AGI-1"]
    B["Design tasks with greater complexity and generalization difficulty than ARC-AGI-1"] --> C["Normalize the distribution of task difficulty across the dataset"]
    C["Normalize the distribution of task difficulty across the dataset"] --> D["Validate each task with 2 to 10 independent non-expert human testers"]
    D["Validate each task with 2 to 10 independent non-expert human testers"] --> E["Select only tasks solved by at least two independent human testers"]
    E["Select only tasks solved by at least two independent human testers"] --> F["Split into 400 public training, 120 semi-private, and 120 private evaluation tasks"]
    F["Split into 400 public training, 120 semi-private, and 120 private evaluation tasks"] --> G["Report state-of-the-art scores only on semi-private and private evaluation sets"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["由人类创建具有独特逻辑的任务需独立规则发现"] --> B["设计比ARC-AGI-1更复杂和泛化难度更高的任务"]
    B["设计比ARC-AGI-1更复杂和泛化难度更高的任务"] --> C["归一化数据集中任务难度的分布"]
    C["归一化数据集中任务难度的分布"] --> D["用2到10名独立非专家人类测试者验证每个任务"]
    D["用2到10名独立非专家人类测试者验证每个任务"] --> E["仅选择至少两名独立测试者解决的任务"]
    E["仅选择至少两名独立测试者解决的任务"] --> F["拆分为400个公开训练、120个半私有和120个私有评估任务"]
    F["拆分为400个公开训练、120个半私有和120个私有评估任务"] --> G["仅在半私有和私有评估集上报告最先进分数"]

name: Arena-Hard-Auto
intro: Arena-Hard-Auto 是一个自动化构建的基准测试，它利用 BenchBuilder 流水线从大规模众包数据（如 Chatbot Arena）中筛选出500个具有挑战性的开放式提示。该基准旨在通过LLM作为裁判进行自动化评估，以衡量和区分前沿语言模型的性能，并确保与人类偏好高度相关。
build_method: 混合, 众包, LLM生成
intro_en: Arena-Hard-Auto is an automatically constructed benchmark that uses the BenchBuilder pipeline to select 500 challenging open-ended prompts from large-scale crowdsourced data such as Chatbot Arena. This benchmark aims to measure and differentiate the performance of state-of-the-art language models through automated evaluation with LLMs as judges, ensuring high correlation with human preferences.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Large-scale Crowdsourced Data) --> B{{BenchBuilder Pipeline}}
    B --> C[Select Challenging Prompts]
    C --> D{Prompt Quality Filter}
    D -->|Pass| E[LLM Generation/Refinement]
    D -->|Fail| F[Discard Low-Quality Prompts]
    E --> G(500 Open-ended Prompts)
    G --> H(Arena-Hard-Auto Benchmark)
    H --> I[Measure LLM Performance]
    I --> J[Differentiate SOTA Models]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Filter 200,000 Chatbot Arena prompts, removing duplicates, multi-turn conversations, and non-English content"] --> B["Encode prompts with text-embedding-3-small, reduce dimensions with UMAP, and cluster into 4,000 topics using HDBSCAN"]
    B["Encode prompts with text-embedding-3-small, reduce dimensions with UMAP, and cluster into 4,000 topics using HDBSCAN"] --> C["Use GPT-4-Turbo to score each prompt on seven key qualities, producing a quality score"]
    C["Use GPT-4-Turbo to score each prompt on seven key qualities, producing a quality score"] --> D["Discard prompts scoring below 6 and topic clusters with mean score below 5"]
    D["Discard prompts scoring below 6 and topic clusters with mean score below 5"] --> E["Sample 2 prompts from each of 250 randomly selected high-quality clusters to create 500-prompt benchmark"]
    E["Sample 2 prompts from each of 250 randomly selected high-quality clusters to create 500-prompt benchmark"] --> F["Remove personally identifiable information and offensive content from the final dataset"]
    F["Remove personally identifiable information and offensive content from the final dataset"] --> G["Evaluate models using LLM-as-a-Judge on the curated 500 challenging open-ended prompts"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["过滤20万条Arena提示，去除重复、多轮对话和非英语内容"] --> B["用嵌入模型编码提示，UMAP降维后用HDBSCAN聚类为4000个主题"]
    B["用嵌入模型编码提示，UMAP降维后用HDBSCAN聚类为4000个主题"] --> C["用GPT-4-Turbo对每个提示的七项关键质量评分"]
    C["用GPT-4-Turbo对每个提示的七项关键质量评分"] --> D["丢弃评分低于6的提示和均分低于5的主题簇"]
    D["丢弃评分低于6的提示和均分低于5的主题簇"] --> E["从250个高质量主题簇中各抽取2个提示，构建500题基准"]
    E["从250个高质量主题簇中各抽取2个提示，构建500题基准"] --> F["从最终数据集中移除个人身份信息和冒犯性内容"]
    F["从最终数据集中移除个人身份信息和冒犯性内容"] --> G["用LLM裁判在500个挑战性开放提示上自动评估模型"]

name: ART
intro: ART (Automated Red Teaming) 是一个用于自动化红队测试的基准，旨在评估AI系统在面对对抗性攻击时的鲁棒性和安全性。
intro_en: ART (Automated Red Teaming) is a benchmark for automated red teaming tests, designed to evaluate the robustness and security of AI systems against adversarial attacks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Attack Scenarios) --> B[Automated Attack Simulation]
    C(Existing Adversarial Datasets) --> D[Data Aggregation]
    B --> D
    D --> E[Data Structuring & Formatting]
    E --> F{Adversarial Content Filter}
    F -- "Pass" --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    F -- "Fail" --> H[Discard Invalid Data]
    G --> I{{Red Teaming Scenario Generation}}
    I --> J(ART Red Teaming Dataset)
    J --> K[AI System Robustness Evaluation]
    K --> L[Security Metric Reporting]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Analyze existing benchmarks to identify three dominant error categories: retrieval, aggregation, and conditional logic"] --> B["Mine real-world EHR data to identify failure patterns and clinical scenarios across 11 lab codes"]
    B["Mine real-world EHR data to identify failure patterns and clinical scenarios across 11 lab codes"] --> C["Generate 600+ action-based tasks using LLMs targeting threshold evaluation, temporal aggregation, and conditional logic"]
    C["Generate 600+ action-based tasks using LLMs targeting threshold evaluation, temporal aggregation, and conditional logic"] --> D["Conduct human-in-the-loop quality audit with clinician review to ensure task realism and clinical fidelity"]
    D["Conduct human-in-the-loop quality audit with clinician review to ensure task realism and clinical fidelity"] --> E["Evaluate GPT-4o-mini and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on structured tasks with prompt refinement"]
    E["Evaluate GPT-4o-mini and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on structured tasks with prompt refinement"] --> F["Measure retrieval accuracy, aggregation performance, and threshold reasoning across all generated tasks"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["分析现有基准识别三类主要错误:检索、聚合和条件逻辑"] --> B["挖掘真实EHR数据识别11种实验室代码的失败模式和临床场景"]
    B["挖掘真实EHR数据识别11种实验室代码的失败模式和临床场景"] --> C["使用LLM生成600+个针对阈值评估、时间聚合和条件逻辑的任务"]
    C["使用LLM生成600+个针对阈值评估、时间聚合和条件逻辑的任务"] --> D["通过临床医生审查进行人工质量审核以确保任务真实性和临床准确性"]
    D["通过临床医生审查进行人工质量审核以确保任务真实性和临床准确性"] --> E["在结构化任务上评估GPT-4o-mini和Claude 3.5 Sonnet并优化提示"]
    E["在结构化任务上评估GPT-4o-mini和Claude 3.5 Sonnet并优化提示"] --> F["测量所有任务的检索准确率、聚合性能和阈值推理表现"]

name: ArtifactsBench
intro: ArtifactsBench 针对大型语言模型生成的动态交互式视觉作品提出评测基准，弥补传统基准只关注算法正确性而忽视视觉质量和交互性的不足。该框架自动渲染生成作品并通过时间序列截图捕捉动态行为，实现多模态自动评测。
build_method: 自动化多模态评估, MLLM-as-Judge, 人工专家验证
modality: 文本, 图像, 代码, 多模态
eval_feature: 动态交互, 多模态, 自动化
build_method_en: Automated Multimodal Evaluation, MLLM-as-Judge, Manual Expert Verification
intro_en: ArtifactsBench proposes an evaluation benchmark for dynamic interactive visual works generated by large language models, addressing the limitation of traditional benchmarks that focus solely on algorithmic correctness while neglecting visual quality and interactivity. The framework automatically renders generated works and captures dynamic behaviors through time-series screenshots, enabling multimodal automatic evaluation.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Aggregate candidates from expert showcases, SVG/web datasets, case studies, and LLM visual-to-query from screenshots"] --> B["Apply automated filters to remove incomplete, non-visual, duplicate, trivial, or non-interactive items prioritizing dynamics"]
    B["Apply automated filters to remove incomplete, non-visual, duplicate, trivial, or non-interactive items prioritizing dynamics"] --> C["Perform two-stage de-duplication using MinHash with semantic similarity and screenshot perceptual hashing"]
    C["Perform two-stage de-duplication using MinHash with semantic similarity and screenshot perceptual hashing"] --> D["Experts rewrite prompts for clarity; assign difficulty post hoc by 30+ model performance to enforce 30/40/30 split"]
    D["Experts rewrite prompts for clarity; assign difficulty post hoc by 30+ model performance to enforce 30/40/30 split"] --> E["Generate 10-dimension checklists via LLM, then human-refine with Cohen's κ ≥ 0.8 inter-annotator agreement"]
    E["Generate 10-dimension checklists via LLM, then human-refine with Cohen's κ ≥ 0.8 inter-annotator agreement"] --> F["Execute artifacts in sandbox and capture three staged screenshots summarizing before/during/after interaction trajectory"]
    F["Execute artifacts in sandbox and capture three staged screenshots summarizing before/during/after interaction trajectory"] --> G["MLLM-as-Judge scores visual evidence and code using checklist across vision-oriented and code-oriented dimensions"]
    G["MLLM-as-Judge scores visual evidence and code using checklist across vision-oriented and code-oriented dimensions"] --> H["Validate referee with 280-instance human study, then cross-validate full rankings against WebDev Arena preferences"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从专家案例、SVG/网页数据集、案例研究和LLM视觉转查询中聚合候选"] --> B["应用自动过滤器移除不完整、非视觉、重复、简单或非交互项并优先保留动态"]
    B["应用自动过滤器移除不完整、非视觉、重复、简单或非交互项并优先保留动态"] --> C["执行两阶段去重：MinHash语义相似度和截图感知哈希"]
    C["执行两阶段去重：MinHash语义相似度和截图感知哈希"] --> D["专家重写提示词以提高清晰度；根据30+模型表现后验分配难度实现30/40/30分布"]
    D["专家重写提示词以提高清晰度；根据30+模型表现后验分配难度实现30/40/30分布"] --> E["通过LLM生成10维检查表，人工精炼确保标注者间Cohen's κ ≥ 0.8"]
    E["通过LLM生成10维检查表，人工精炼确保标注者间Cohen's κ ≥ 0.8"] --> F["在沙箱中执行作品并捕获交互前中后三阶段截图"]
    F["在沙箱中执行作品并捕获交互前中后三阶段截图"] --> G["MLLM裁判依据检查表对视觉证据和代码的视觉维度与代码维度评分"]
    G["MLLM裁判依据检查表对视觉证据和代码的视觉维度与代码维度评分"] --> H["通过280实例人类研究验证裁判，再与WebDev Arena偏好交叉验证完整排名"]

name: ASPERA
intro: ASPERA是一个评估大型语言模型执行复杂操作能力的框架，包含模拟环境和数据生成引擎，用于创建具有挑战性的任务。其附带的Asper-Bench数据集包含250个任务，用于测试基于自定义助手库的程序生成能力。
build_method: LLM生成, 人工标注, 混合
eval_feature: 领域特定
intro_en: ASPERA is a framework for evaluating large language models' ability to perform complex operations, featuring a simulation environment and a data generation engine to create challenging tasks. Its accompanying Asper-Bench dataset includes 250 tasks designed to test program generation capabilities based on custom assistant libraries.

flowchart LR

    B[Data Generation Engine] --> C

    D --> E{Manual Annotation/Review}

    E -->|Approve| G(Asper-Bench Dataset\n250 Tasks)
    F --> G
    G --> H[Model Evaluation]
    H --> I(Performance Metrics)
    I --> J[Agent Capability Assessment]

flowchart LR
    A["Develop assistant library simulation with documented functions and objects for action execution"] --> B["Guide LLM to generate complex user queries requiring multi-step compositional programs"]

    C["Generate corresponding simulation state and databases for each query"] --> D["Create validation programs to verify functional correctness and task success"]
    D["Create validation programs to verify functional correctness and task success"] --> E["Curate 250 challenging tasks into Asper-Bench evaluation dataset"]
    E["Curate 250 challenging tasks into Asper-Bench evaluation dataset"] --> F["Provide assistant library documentation or require LLMs to explore primitives"]
    F["Provide assistant library documentation or require LLMs to explore primitives"] --> G["Evaluate LLM-generated programs by executing them in the simulated environment"]

    A["开发带文档的助手库模拟环境"] --> B["引导LLM生成需多步组合的复杂查询"]
    B["引导LLM生成需多步组合的复杂查询"] --> C["为每个查询生成对应的模拟状态和数据库"]
    C["为每个查询生成对应的模拟状态和数据库"] --> D["创建验证程序以检查功能正确性"]
    D["创建验证程序以检查功能正确性"] --> E["整理250个挑战性任务为评估数据集"]
    E["整理250个挑战性任务为评估数据集"] --> F["提供助手库文档或要求LLM探索原语"]
    F["提供助手库文档或要求LLM探索原语"] --> G["在模拟环境中执行LLM生成的程序并评估"]

name: AstaBench
intro: AstaBench是一个旨在严格评估AI代理科学研究能力的评测套件。它包含超过2400个问题，覆盖科学发现的全过程和多个学科领域，并提供一个配备了生产级搜索工具的可控、可复现的评测环境。该基准还提供九类科学优化的Asta代理和众多基线模型，以促进对AI代理核心能力的公平比较。
task_type: Agent任务, 代码生成, Data Analysis
task_type_en: Agent Task, Code Generation, Data Analysis
intro_en: AstaBench is a benchmark suite designed to rigorously evaluate AI agents' scientific research capabilities. It includes over 2,400 questions covering the entire scientific discovery process across multiple disciplines and offers a controllable, reproducible evaluation environment equipped with production-level search tools. The benchmark also provides nine types of science-optimized Asta agents and numerous baseline models to facilitate fair comparisons of core AI agent abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Scientific Research Domains) --> B[Expert Knowledge & Input]
    A --> C(Existing Scientific Literature)
    B --> D[Question Formulation]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Multi-Disciplinary Coverage]
    E --> F[Structure & Refine Questions]
    F --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(AstaBench Question Set\n2,400 questions)
    H -->|Fail| F
    I --> J[AI Agent Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect problems inspired by actual user requests from deployed Asta agents"] --> B["Construct 2400+ problems spanning full scientific discovery process across multiple domains"]
    B["Construct 2400+ problems spanning full scientific discovery process across multiple domains"] --> C["Build scientific research environment with production-grade search tools for controlled evaluation"]
    C["Build scientific research environment with production-grade search tools for controlled evaluation"] --> D["Develop nine science-optimized classes of Asta agents and numerous baseline agents"]
    D["Develop nine science-optimized classes of Asta agents and numerous baseline agents"] --> E["Provide standardized interfaces for agent prototyping and evaluation on the suite"]
    E["Provide standardized interfaces for agent prototyping and evaluation on the suite"] --> F["Evaluate 57 agents across 22 agent classes on the benchmark suite"]
    F["Evaluate 57 agents across 22 agent classes on the benchmark suite"] --> G["Account for confounding variables including model cost and tool access during evaluation"]

flowchart LR
    A["收集来自已部署Asta代理的真实用户请求"] --> B["构建2400+个覆盖完整科学发现流程的问题"]
    B["构建2400+个覆盖完整科学发现流程的问题"] --> C["构建配备生产级搜索工具的科研环境"]
    C["构建配备生产级搜索工具的科研环境"] --> D["开发九类科学优化Asta代理及众多基线"]
    D["开发九类科学优化Asta代理及众多基线"] --> E["提供标准化接口用于代理原型开发与评测"]
    E["提供标准化接口用于代理原型开发与评测"] --> F["在基准套件上评估22类共57个代理"]
    F["在基准套件上评估22类共57个代理"] --> G["评估中考虑模型成本和工具访问等混淆变量"]

name: AudioMC
intro: Audio MultiChallenge是一个开源基准，旨在评估端到端语音对话系统在自然多轮交互中的表现。该基准基于文本MultiChallenge框架，并新增了“语音编辑”维度，以测试模型对语音修正和回溯的鲁棒性。它包含452段由47名说话人参与的对话，揭示了当前先进模型在处理编辑追踪、音频线索和长程上下文方面的挑战。
build_method: 混合, 人工标注, Agentic生成
eval_feature: 多轮, 长文本, 语音编辑
intro_en: Audio MultiChallenge is an open-source benchmark designed to evaluate end-to-end speech dialogue systems in natural multi-turn interactions. Built on the text-based MultiChallenge framework, it introduces a "speech editing" dimension to test models' robustness in speech correction and backtracking. It includes 452 dialogues involving 47 speakers, highlighting current state-of-the-art models' challenges in handling edit tracking, audio cues, and long-range context.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Text-based MultiChallenge Framework) --> B{{Agentic Dialogue Generation}}
    B --> C[Speech Synthesis]
    C --> D[Introduce Speech Editing Scenarios]
    D --> E(Raw Audio Data)
    E --> F{{Human Annotation}}
    F --> G{Quality Check}
    G -->|Pass| H(Curated AudioMC Dataset)
    G -->|Fail| I[Review & Refine]
    I --> F
    H --> J[Evaluate Speech Dialogue Systems]
    J --> K[Test Robustness (Correction/Backtracking)]
metric_en: Average Pass Rate (APR), Average Rubric Score (ARS)

flowchart LR
    A["Adapt text-based MultiChallenge axes to audio modality with new Voice Editing dimension"] --> B["Generate multi-turn conversation blueprints using multi-agent synthetic component"]
    B["Generate multi-turn conversation blueprints using multi-agent synthetic component"] --> C["Record 452 unscripted conversations from 47 speakers via human-in-the-loop process"]
    C["Record 452 unscripted conversations from 47 speakers via human-in-the-loop process"] --> D["Preserve natural disfluencies, speech repairs, and paralinguistic signals in audio"]
    D["Preserve natural disfluencies, speech repairs, and paralinguistic signals in audio"] --> E["Decompose each ideal response into 1,712 atomic instance-specific rubrics"]

    F["Validate rubrics with human evaluators achieving 93% agreement rate"] --> G["Evaluate models using LLM-as-a-judge requiring all rubrics satisfied to pass"]
