name: FACTS Grounding
intro: FACTS Grounding 是一个在线排行榜和基准测试，旨在评估语言模型根据用户提示中提供的上下文生成事实准确文本的能力。该基准测试中的每个提示都包含一个用户请求和一个最长为 32k 词符的完整文档，要求模型生成完全基于所提供上下文的长篇回答。模型使用自动化的评判模型分两个阶段进行评估，确保回答既满足用户请求又完全基于所提供的文档。
org: Google DeepMind, Google Research, Google Cloud, Kaggle
intro_en: FACTS Grounding is an online leaderboard and benchmark designed to evaluate language models' ability to generate factually accurate text based on context provided in user prompts. Each prompt in this benchmark includes a user request and a full document up to 32k tokens, requiring the model to produce a long-form response entirely grounded in the given context. Models are evaluated in two stages using an automated judging model to ensure responses both satisfy the user request and are fully based on the provided document.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(User Prompts) --> C[Manual Prompt & Document Curation]
    B(Source Documents) --> C
    C --> D[Contextualization & Factual Alignment]
    D --> E{Content Review}
    E -->|Pass| F[Expert Annotation & Validation]
    E -->|Fail| G[Discard Invalid Data]
    F --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(FACTS Grounding Dataset)
    H -->|Fail| F
    I --> J[Language Model Inference]
    J --> K[Factual Accuracy Evaluation]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
    L --> M[Online Leaderboard Update]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Third-party raters create prompts pairing web documents with non-trivial user requests across enterprise domains"] --> B["Manually verify all examples to remove creative tasks, OCR-unreadable PDFs, and ensure context-only reliance"]
    B["Manually verify all examples to remove creative tasks, OCR-unreadable PDFs, and ensure context-only reliance"] --> C["Test seven prompt templates on held-out set with golden labels; select best per judge via Macro-F1"]
    C["Test seven prompt templates on held-out set with golden labels; select best per judge via Macro-F1"] --> D["Apply three judge models to produce binary grounding labels for each model response"]
    D["Apply three judge models to produce binary grounding labels for each model response"] --> E["Disqualify responses that do not sufficiently fulfill the user request before factuality scoring"]
    E["Disqualify responses that do not sufficiently fulfill the user request before factuality scoring"] --> F["Calculate unadjusted factuality score as average of three judges' accurate response percentages"]
    F["Calculate unadjusted factuality score as average of three judges' accurate response percentages"] --> G["Construct public and private leaderboard splits using balanced random sampling strategy"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["第三方标注员创建提示，将网络文档与企业领域的非平凡用户请求配对"] --> B["人工验证所有样本，移除创意任务和OCR不可读PDF，确保仅依赖上下文"]
    B["人工验证所有样本，移除创意任务和OCR不可读PDF，确保仅依赖上下文"] --> C["在留出集上测试七个提示模板，通过宏F1为每个评判器选择最佳模板"]
    C["在留出集上测试七个提示模板，通过宏F1为每个评判器选择最佳模板"] --> D["应用三个评判模型为每个模型响应生成二元立足性标签"]
    D["应用三个评判模型为每个模型响应生成二元立足性标签"] --> E["在事实性评分前取消不充分满足用户请求的响应资格"]
    E["在事实性评分前取消不充分满足用户请求的响应资格"] --> F["计算未调整事实性分数为三个评判器准确响应百分比的平均值"]
    F["计算未调整事实性分数为三个评判器准确响应百分比的平均值"] --> G["使用平衡随机采样策略构建公开和私有排行榜划分"]

name: FEA-Bench
intro: FEA-Bench是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）在代码仓库中执行增量开发能力的基准。它通过收集来自83个GitHub仓库的拉取请求，并利用基于规则和意图的过滤来构建专注于新功能开发任务实例。每个任务实例都配有相关的单元测试文件，以确保解决方案可以被验证，从而对LLM的自动化软件工程能力提供更全面的评估。
build_method: 网络爬取, 基于规则和意图的过滤
task_type: 代码生成, 代码编辑, 代码补全
task_type_en: Code Generation, Code Editing, Code Completion
build_method_en: Web Crawling, Rule- and Intent-based Filtering
intro_en: FEA-Bench is a benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) ability to perform incremental development in code repositories. It collects pull requests from 83 GitHub repositories and uses rule-based and intent-based filtering to create task instances focused on new feature development. Each task instance includes associated unit test files to ensure solutions can be validated, providing a more comprehensive assessment of LLMs' automated software engineering capabilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[GitHub Repositories] --> B{{Web Crawling}}
    B --> C(Raw Pull Requests)
    C --> D{Rule-based Filtering}
    D -->|Pass Rules| E(Rule-Filtered Pull Requests)
    D -->|Fail Rules| K[Discard Irrelevant PRs]
    E --> F{Intent-based Filtering}
    F -->|Focus on New Features| G(New Feature Development Instances)
    F -->|Not New Feature| L[Discard Other PRs]
    G --> H(FEA-Bench Dataset
LLM Incremental Development Tasks)
    H --> I[LLM Evaluation]
    I --> J[Benchmark Results]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect GitHub repositories from Top PyPI with licenses and over 1,000 pull requests"] --> B["Extract pull requests that include test file changes and validate using pytest"]
    B["Extract pull requests that include test file changes and validate using pytest"] --> C["Apply rule-based and intent-based filtering to select feature development task instances"]
    C["Apply rule-based and intent-based filtering to select feature development task instances"] --> D["Pair each task with feature request, new component definitions, and environment setup"]
    D["Pair each task with feature request, new component definitions, and environment setup"] --> E["Extract gold patch and test patch from pull request code changes"]
    E["Extract gold patch and test patch from pull request code changes"] --> F["Run pytest before and after applying gold patch to establish ground truth"]
    F["Run pytest before and after applying gold patch to establish ground truth"] --> G["Assemble 1,401 task instances from 83 repositories with paired unit tests"]
    G["Assemble 1,401 task instances from 83 repositories with paired unit tests"] --> H["Evaluate LLM-generated code by executing unit tests in configured environments"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从Top PyPI收集有许可证且超过1000个PR的仓库"] --> B["提取包含测试文件变更的PR并用pytest验证"]
    B["提取包含测试文件变更的PR并用pytest验证"] --> C["应用基于规则和意图的过滤选出功能开发任务实例"]
    C["应用基于规则和意图的过滤选出功能开发任务实例"] --> D["为每个任务配对功能请求、新组件定义和环境配置"]
    D["为每个任务配对功能请求、新组件定义和环境配置"] --> E["从PR代码变更中提取金标准补丁和测试补丁"]
    E["从PR代码变更中提取金标准补丁和测试补丁"] --> F["应用金标准补丁前后运行pytest建立真值标准"]
    F["应用金标准补丁前后运行pytest建立真值标准"] --> G["从83个仓库组装1401个带配对单元测试的任务实例"]
    G["从83个仓库组装1401个带配对单元测试的任务实例"] --> H["在配置环境中执行单元测试评估LLM生成代码"]

name: FinSearchComp
intro: FinSearchComp是一个完全开源的Agent基准测试，旨在对金融搜索和推理能力进行真实、专家级别的评估。该基准包含三个任务：时间敏感数据获取、简单历史查询和复杂历史调查，以复现真实的金融分析师工作流程。通过70位专业金融专家的标注和严格的质量保证流程，确保了其难度和可靠性。它包含635个问题，涵盖全球和大中华区市场，并评估了21个模型。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: FinSearchComp is a fully open-source Agent benchmark designed for realistic, expert-level evaluation of financial search and reasoning capabilities. It includes three tasks: time-sensitive data retrieval, simple historical queries, and complex historical investigations, replicating real financial analyst workflows. With annotations from 70 professional financial experts and a rigorous quality assurance process, it ensures both difficulty and reliability. The benchmark contains 635 questions covering global and Greater China markets and evaluates 21 models.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Financial Data Sources) --> B[Expert Data Identification]
    B --> C[Data Retrieval & Collection]
    C --> D[Task Scenario Design]
    D --> E[Data Structuring for Tasks]
    E --> F{{Manual Annotation}}
    F --> G{{Expert Review & Validation}}
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -- Fail --> I[Data Refinement]
    I --> D
    H -- Pass --> J(FinSearchComp Benchmark Dataset)
    J --> K[Agent Model Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Design three task families mirroring analyst workflows: Time-Sensitive Fetching, Simple Lookup, Complex Investigation"] --> B["Engage 70 professional financial experts to annotate 635 questions across global and Greater China markets"]
    B["Engage 70 professional financial experts to annotate 635 questions across global and Greater China markets"] --> C["Conduct multi-stage verification of prompts and reference answers to ensure difficulty and reliability"]
    C["Conduct multi-stage verification of prompts and reference answers to ensure difficulty and reliability"] --> D["Specify detailed rubric-based scoring guidelines with explicit tolerance bands for minor fluctuations"]
    D["Specify detailed rubric-based scoring guidelines with explicit tolerance bands for minor fluctuations"] --> E["Evaluate 21 models using LLM-based judging with rubric-guided assessment and tolerance bands"]
    E["Evaluate 21 models using LLM-based judging with rubric-guided assessment and tolerance bands"] --> F["Perform expert spot checks to validate overall correctness of LLM-based evaluation results"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["设计三类任务模拟分析师工作流：时间敏感获取、简单查询、复杂调查"] --> B["邀请70位金融专家标注635个全球和大中华区市场问题"]
    B["邀请70位金融专家标注635个全球和大中华区市场问题"] --> C["对提示和参考答案进行多阶段验证以确保难度和可靠性"]
    C["对提示和参考答案进行多阶段验证以确保难度和可靠性"] --> D["制定详细的评分标准并设置容差范围以应对细微波动"]
    D["制定详细的评分标准并设置容差范围以应对细微波动"] --> E["使用基于LLM的评判和容差范围评估21个模型"]
    E["使用基于LLM的评判和容差范围评估21个模型"] --> F["进行专家抽查以验证LLM评估结果的整体正确性"]

name: FIREBENCH
intro: FIREBENCH 是一个面向企业与 API 场景的指令遵循评测基准，围绕输出格式、内容约束和流程性要求评估模型在生产环境中的可靠性。
build_method: 企业场景任务设计, 程序化验证, 细粒度评分规则
task_type: 指令遵循, 信息抽取, 客服, 代码代理
eval_feature: 企业场景, 格式约束, API驱动
intro_en: FIREBENCH is an instruction-following benchmark for enterprise and API-driven LLM applications with strict output and procedural constraints.
build_method_en: Enterprise task design, programmatic verification, fine-grained scoring rules
task_type_en: Instruction Following, Information Extraction, Customer Support, Coding Agents
eval_feature_en: Enterprise Scenarios, Format Constraints, API-driven Evaluation
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Sample 100 questions from long-document and reasoning benchmarks, evaluate under 21 format constraints programmatically"] --> B["Sample 100 coding problems from MHPP, require separation into reasoning, code, and explanation under three formats"]
    B["Sample 100 coding problems from MHPP, require separation into reasoning, code, and explanation under three formats"] --> C["Simulate 200 customer support sessions with dual-role models collecting 10–15 fields in strict sequential order"]
    C["Simulate 200 customer support sessions with dual-role models collecting 10–15 fields in strict sequential order"] --> D["Construct 20 realistic tables, evaluate 200 trials requiring top-N row retrieval by specified sorting criteria"]
    D["Construct 20 realistic tables, evaluate 200 trials requiring top-N row retrieval by specified sorting criteria"] --> E["Sample 300 challenging questions, test with standard and uncertainty-aware prompts to measure appropriate refusal behavior"]
    E["Sample 300 challenging questions, test with standard and uncertainty-aware prompts to measure appropriate refusal behavior"] --> F["Manually curate 70 unanswerable questions over long documents requiring explicit refusal when information is insufficient"]
    F["Manually curate 70 unanswerable questions over long documents requiring explicit refusal when information is insufficient"] --> G["Augment Arena Hard 2.0 prompts with mandatory positive content requirements, evaluate via LLM judge with rubrics"]
    G["Augment Arena Hard 2.0 prompts with mandatory positive content requirements, evaluate via LLM judge with rubrics"] --> H["Augment same prompts with prohibitive negative constraints, evaluate avoidance of forbidden elements via LLM judge"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从长文档和推理基准采样100题，在21种格式约束下程序化验证"] --> B["从MHPP采样100个编程问题，要求分离推理、代码和解释为三种格式"]
    B["从MHPP采样100个编程问题，要求分离推理、代码和解释为三种格式"] --> C["模拟200个客服会话，双角色模型按严格顺序收集10至15个字段"]
    C["模拟200个客服会话，双角色模型按严格顺序收集10至15个字段"] --> D["构建20个真实表格，评估200次试验要求按指定标准返回前N行"]
    D["构建20个真实表格，评估200次试验要求按指定标准返回前N行"] --> E["采样300个挑战性问题，用标准和不确定性提示测试适当拒答行为"]
    E["采样300个挑战性问题，用标准和不确定性提示测试适当拒答行为"] --> F["人工策划70个长文档不可答问题，要求信息不足时明确拒答"]
    F["人工策划70个长文档不可答问题，要求信息不足时明确拒答"] --> G["为Arena Hard 2.0提示增加强制正向内容要求，用评分标准和LLM评判"]
    G["为Arena Hard 2.0提示增加强制正向内容要求，用评分标准和LLM评判"] --> H["为相同提示增加禁止性负向约束，用LLM评判评估对禁止元素的规避"]

name: FLAMES
intro: FLAMES是一个针对大语言模型价值观对齐的中文评测基准，由上海人工智能实验室和复旦大学共同开发。它包含一套精心设计的对抗性提示，旨在揭示模型在安全性、公平性等方面的深层价值观对齐缺陷。该基准不仅涵盖了通用的无害原则，还融入了和谐等具有中国文化特色的价值观维度。
org: Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, Fudan University
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 安全测试, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 对抗性, 领域特定
intro_en: FLAMES is a Chinese evaluation benchmark for value alignment in large language models, jointly developed by Shanghai AI Laboratory and Fudan University. It includes a set of carefully designed adversarial prompts aimed at revealing deep value alignment flaws in areas such as safety and fairness. The benchmark covers general harmlessness principles and incorporates culturally specific values like harmony.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(LLM Safety & Alignment Principles) --> B[Expert Knowledge Elicitation]
    B --> C[Adversarial Prompt Design]
    C --> D{{Prompt Generation Tools}}
    D --> E[Initial Prompt Set]
    E --> F{Prompt Effectiveness Check}
    F -- Fail --> G[Refine Prompts]
    G --> C
    F -- Pass --> H[Manual Annotation & Review]
    H --> I[Categorize Prompts (Safety/Fairness)]
    I --> J(FLAMES Adversarial Prompt Dataset)
    J --> K[LLM Evaluation]
    K --> L[Value Alignment Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Design adversarial prompts using complex scenarios and jailbreaking methods with implicit malice"] --> B["Organize prompts across five dimensions: Fairness, Legality, Data protection, Morality, and Safety"]
    B["Organize prompts across five dimensions: Fairness, Legality, Data protection, Morality, and Safety"] --> C["Integrate Chinese-specific values such as harmony into the morality dimension"]
    C["Integrate Chinese-specific values such as harmony into the morality dimension"] --> D["Prompt 17 mainstream LLMs with the designed adversarial prompts to collect responses"]
    D["Prompt 17 mainstream LLMs with the designed adversarial prompts to collect responses"] --> E["Rigorously annotate model responses with fine-grained labels for detailed evaluation"]
    E["Rigorously annotate model responses with fine-grained labels for detailed evaluation"] --> F["Train a lightweight specified scorer on annotated data for multi-dimensional scoring"]
    F["Train a lightweight specified scorer on annotated data for multi-dimensional scoring"] --> G["Evaluate LLMs across all dimensions using the trained scorer"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["设计包含复杂场景和越狱方法的对抗性提示"] --> B["将提示按公平、合法、数据保护、道德和安全五个维度组织"]
    B["将提示按公平、合法、数据保护、道德和安全五个维度组织"] --> C["将和谐等中国特色价值观融入道德维度"]
    C["将和谐等中国特色价值观融入道德维度"] --> D["用设计的对抗性提示测试17个主流大模型并收集回复"]
    D["用设计的对抗性提示测试17个主流大模型并收集回复"] --> E["对模型回复进行细粒度标注以支持详细评估"]
    E["对模型回复进行细粒度标注以支持详细评估"] --> F["在标注数据上训练轻量级评分器进行多维度打分"]
    F["在标注数据上训练轻量级评分器进行多维度打分"] --> G["使用训练好的评分器对大模型进行全维度评估"]

name: FOFO
intro: 本文提出了FOFO，一个开创性的基准，用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）遵循复杂、特定领域格式的能力，这是它们作为AI代理应用的一个关键但未被充分研究的能力。FOFO通过AI与人类协作的方法，开发了多样的真实世界格式和指令，填补了现有基准在评估格式遵循能力方面的空白。
org: Salesforce Research, University of Illinois at Chicago, Pennsylvania State University
task_type: 格式化文本生成, 指令遵循
intro_en: This paper introduces FOFO, a pioneering benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) ability to follow complex, domain-specific formats—a critical yet underexplored capability for their use as AI agents. FOFO employs AI-human collaboration to develop diverse real-world formats and instructions, addressing the gap in existing benchmarks for assessing format adherence.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Domain experts identify initial subdomains where LLM agents show potential"] --> B["Prompt GPT-4 to expand subdomain list and summarize into super domains"]
    B["Prompt GPT-4 to expand subdomain list and summarize into super domains"] --> C["Human experts review and iteratively refine domains and subdomains with GPT-4"]
    C["Human experts review and iteratively refine domains and subdomains with GPT-4"] --> D["GPT-4 generates five text-only, domain-specific formats per subdomain, excluding generic formats"]
    D["GPT-4 generates five text-only, domain-specific formats per subdomain, excluding generic formats"] --> E["Human experts assess format quality and regenerate or remove misaligned formats"]
    E["Human experts assess format quality and regenerate or remove misaligned formats"] --> F["Generate format-oriented instructions with complex requirements for each domain-subdomain-format triplet"]
    F["Generate format-oriented instructions with complex requirements for each domain-subdomain-format triplet"] --> G["Test diverse LLMs on format instructions using GPT-4 as judge"]
    G["Test diverse LLMs on format instructions using GPT-4 as judge"] --> H["Conduct human evaluation to validate high GPT-4-human agreement on judgments"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["领域专家识别LLM代理有潜力的初始子领域"] --> B["提示GPT-4扩展子领域列表并总结为超级领域"]
    B["提示GPT-4扩展子领域列表并总结为超级领域"] --> C["人类专家审查并与GPT-4迭代优化领域和子领域"]
    C["人类专家审查并与GPT-4迭代优化领域和子领域"] --> D["GPT-4为每个子领域生成五种文本专用格式,排除通用格式"]
    D["GPT-4为每个子领域生成五种文本专用格式,排除通用格式"] --> E["人类专家评估格式质量并重新生成或删除不符合的格式"]
    E["人类专家评估格式质量并重新生成或删除不符合的格式"] --> F["为每个领域-子领域-格式三元组生成复杂要求的格式指令"]
    F["为每个领域-子领域-格式三元组生成复杂要求的格式指令"] --> G["使用GPT-4作为评判测试多种LLM的格式指令表现"]
    G["使用GPT-4作为评判测试多种LLM的格式指令表现"] --> H["进行人工评估以验证GPT-4与人类判断的高度一致性"]

name: FRAMES
intro: FRAMES（Factuality, Retrieval, And reasoning MEasurement Set）是一个高质量数据集，旨在测试大型语言模型（LLMs）在生成最终答案时的事实性、检索能力和推理能力。该基准测试提供了一个统一的框架，用于评估LLMs在端到端检索增强生成（RAG）场景中的性能。FRAMES包含具有挑战性的多跳问题，需要整合来自多个来源的信息。
org: Harvard University, Google DeepMind, New York University, Duke University, University of Massachuset
build_method: 人工标注, 混合
eval_feature: 多跳, 过程评估
intro_en: FRAMES (Factuality, Retrieval, And reasoning MEasurement Set) is a high-quality dataset designed to test large language models (LLMs) on factuality, retrieval ability, and reasoning when generating final answers. This benchmark provides a unified framework to evaluate LLMs' performance in end-to-end retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) scenarios. FRAMES includes challenging multi-hop questions that require integrating information from multiple sources.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Initial Data Sources] --> B[Task Definition & Design]
    B --> C{{Hybrid Data Generation}}
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E{Annotation Review & Conflict Resolution}
    E -->|Pass| F[Data Cleaning & Formatting]
    E -->|Fail| D
    F --> G[Expert Validation]
    G --> H(FRAMES Dataset\nFactuality, Retrieval, Reasoning)
    H --> I[LLM Answer Generation]
    I --> J[Automated & Manual Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Prompt LLM to generate multi-article questions; observe over 30% hallucination rate in outputs"] --> B["Manually clean hallucinated samples and test LLM accuracy on cleaned questions"]
    B["Manually clean hallucinated samples and test LLM accuracy on cleaned questions"] --> C["Human annotators create 824 questions requiring 2-15 Wikipedia articles with reasoning type labels"]
    C["Human annotators create 824 questions requiring 2-15 Wikipedia articles with reasoning type labels"] --> D["Re-annotate questions three months later to verify correctness and filter outdated answers"]
    D["Re-annotate questions three months later to verify correctness and filter outdated answers"] --> E["Add temporal context to questions and remove binary answers to prevent guessing"]
    E["Add temporal context to questions and remove binary answers to prevent guessing"] --> F["Evaluate models with naive prompts, BM25 retrieval at varying document counts, and oracle prompts"]
    F["Evaluate models with naive prompts, BM25 retrieval at varying document counts, and oracle prompts"] --> G["Conduct multi-step evaluations forcing models to retrieve and reason across multiple inference steps"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["提示LLM生成多文章问题，发现超30%幻觉率"] --> B["人工清理幻觉样本并测试清理后问题的准确率"]
    B["人工清理幻觉样本并测试清理后问题的准确率"] --> C["人工标注员创建824个需2-15篇维基文章的问题并标注推理类型"]
    C["人工标注员创建824个需2-15篇维基文章的问题并标注推理类型"] --> D["三个月后重新标注问题以验证正确性并过滤过时答案"]
    D["三个月后重新标注问题以验证正确性并过滤过时答案"] --> E["为问题添加时间上下文并移除二元答案以防猜测"]
    E["为问题添加时间上下文并移除二元答案以防猜测"] --> F["用朴素提示、不同文档数的BM25检索和预言提示评估模型"]
    F["用朴素提示、不同文档数的BM25检索和预言提示评估模型"] --> G["进行多步评估迫使模型在多个推理步骤中检索和推理"]

name: FronTalk
intro: FronTalk 是一个面向前端开发的多轮对话式代码生成基准，强调文本与视觉反馈共同驱动的多轮实现过程，并通过智能体式网页探索评估功能正确性与用户体验。
build_method: 真实网站多轮对话整理, 用户模拟器生成文本和视觉指令, 人工精炼测试用例
modality: 文本, 图像, 代码
task_type: 前端代码生成, 多轮软件工程
eval_feature: 多模态反馈, 智能体评估, 遗忘分析
intro_en: FronTalk is a front-end development benchmark for conversational code generation with multi-turn interaction and multimodal feedback.
build_method_en: Curated multi-turn dialogues from real websites, generated textual and visual instructions with user simulators, manually refined test cases
task_type_en: Front-end Code Generation, Multi-turn Software Engineering
eval_feature_en: Multimodal Feedback, Agent-based Evaluation, Forgetting Analysis
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Sample 10,000 webpages from C4, cluster summaries with BERTopic, select 100 diverse websites"] --> B["Prompt GPT-4o to generate user intents and test cases for functionality and design turns"]
    B["Prompt GPT-4o to generate user intents and test cases for functionality and design turns"] --> C["Manually refine all test cases to ensure they are hallucination-free, unambiguous, and verifiable"]
    C["Manually refine all test cases to ensure they are hallucination-free, unambiguous, and verifiable"] --> D["Deploy textual and visual user simulators to contextualize intents into instructions with drawing tools"]
    D["Deploy textual and visual user simulators to contextualize intents into instructions with drawing tools"] --> E["Generate multi-turn code outputs conditioned on conversation history and simulated user instructions"]
    E["Generate multi-turn code outputs conditioned on conversation history and simulated user instructions"] --> F["Render final code into website and deploy web agent with image manipulation tools"]
    F["Render final code into website and deploy web agent with image manipulation tools"] --> G["Agent interacts with website to verify test cases and measure pass rate and usability"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从C4采样1万网页，用BERTopic聚类摘要，选出100个多样化网站"] --> B["用GPT-4o生成功能和设计类用户意图及测试用例"]
    B["用GPT-4o生成功能和设计类用户意图及测试用例"] --> C["人工精炼所有测试用例，确保无幻觉、无歧义且可验证"]
    C["人工精炼所有测试用例，确保无幻觉、无歧义且可验证"] --> D["部署文本和视觉用户模拟器，用绘图工具将意图情境化为指令"]
    D["部署文本和视觉用户模拟器，用绘图工具将意图情境化为指令"] --> E["基于对话历史和模拟用户指令生成多轮代码输出"]
    E["基于对话历史和模拟用户指令生成多轮代码输出"] --> F["将最终代码渲染为网站并部署带图像处理工具的网页智能体"]
    F["将最终代码渲染为网站并部署带图像处理工具的网页智能体"] --> G["智能体与网站交互验证测试用例并测量通过率和可用性"]

name: FrontierMath
intro: FrontierMath 是一个面向高级数学推理的高难度基准，收录由专家数学家原创并审校的未发表题目，并通过自动验证降低数据污染风险。
build_method: 专家数学家原创命题, 专家审校, 自动验证
eval_feature: 未发表题目, 自动验证, 低污染风险, 分层难度
intro_en: FrontierMath is a benchmark for advanced mathematical reasoning built from original unpublished problems crafted and vetted by expert mathematicians.
build_method_en: expert mathematician problem writing, expert vetting, automated verification
eval_feature_en: Unpublished Problems, Automated Verification, Low Contamination Risk, Tiered Difficulty
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["邀请专家数学家围绕现代数学主要分支原创命题"] --> B["对题目做专家审校与原创性检查"]
    B --> C["按 Tier 1-4 与开放问题组织难度结构"]
    C --> D["为每道题准备可自动核验的标准答案或验证程序"]
    D --> E["仅保留未发表且高难度的问题以降低污染风险"]
    E --> F["在完整题集上评测前沿模型的高级数学推理能力"]
    F --> G["按 success rate 跟踪模型在专家级数学问题上的进展"]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Invite expert mathematicians to write original problems across major branches of modern mathematics"] --> B["Vet the problems through expert review and originality checks"]
    B --> C["Organize difficulty into Tiers 1-4 plus open problems"]
    C --> D["Prepare automatically verifiable answers or verification procedures"]
    D --> E["Keep the problems unpublished and exceptionally difficult to reduce contamination risk"]
    E --> F["Evaluate frontier models on the full benchmark of advanced mathematical reasoning"]
    F --> G["Track progress with success rate on expert-level math problems"]

name: FrontierScience
intro: FrontierScience是一个评估前沿语言模型专家级科学推理能力的基准测试。它通过奥林匹克竞赛问题和博士级别的开放式研究问题两个互补的轨道来解决现有科学基准的不足。该基准包含数百个问题，涵盖物理、化学和生物学等子领域，并由国际奥林匹克奖牌获得者和博士科学家构建。它引入了基于细致评分标准的评估框架，以评估模型在解决研究任务过程中的能力。
org: OpenAI, Harvard University, University of Washington
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 开放式问答, 选择题
scale: 数百个问题（包括160个开源黄金集）
intro_en: FrontierScience is a benchmark that evaluates cutting-edge language models' expert-level scientific reasoning abilities. It addresses the limitations of existing science benchmarks through two complementary tracks: Olympiad competition problems and open-ended doctoral-level research questions. The benchmark includes hundreds of questions across subfields such as physics, chemistry, and biology, developed by international Olympiad medalists and PhD scientists. It introduces a fine-grained scoring-based evaluation framework to assess models' capabilities in tackling research tasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Olympiad Competition Problems) --> C[Expert Problem Formulation]
    B(Doctoral-level Research Questions) --> C
    C --> D[Content Refinement & Structuring]
    D --> E{{Manual Annotation}}
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -- Pass --> H(FrontierScience Benchmark Dataset)
    F -- Fail --> G[Iterative Revision]
    G --> D
    H --> I[Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Scientific Reasoning Assessment]
scale_en: Hundreds of questions (including 160 items open-source gold set)
metric_en: Evaluation based on detailed scoring criteria
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["International olympiad medalists write novel Olympiad-style problems with closed-form numeric or algebraic answers"] --> B["PhD scientists write original research sub-tasks requiring three to five hours, grounded in active research areas"]
    B["PhD scientists write original research sub-tasks requiring three to five hours, grounded in active research areas"] --> C["Independent domain experts review each question, answer, and solution for correctness and guideline adherence"]
    C["Independent domain experts review each question, answer, and solution for correctness and guideline adherence"] --> D["Disagreements between writer and reviewer are resolved through consensus or question is discarded"]
    D["Disagreements between writer and reviewer are resolved through consensus or question is discarded"] --> E["Meta-review filters over 500 Olympiad and 200 Research questions to gold set of 100 and 60"]
    E["Meta-review filters over 500 Olympiad and 200 Research questions to gold set of 100 and 60"] --> F["Research questions paired with 10-point rubrics assessing intermediate reasoning steps and final answer correctness"]
    F["Research questions paired with 10-point rubrics assessing intermediate reasoning steps and final answer correctness"] --> G["Olympiad answers graded via expression equivalence or fuzzy string match across 20 independent trials"]
    G["Olympiad answers graded via expression equivalence or fuzzy string match across 20 independent trials"] --> H["Research answers judged by GPT-5 model assigning rubric points, averaged over 30 trials with 7-point threshold"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["国际奥赛奖牌得主撰写新颖的奥赛风格问题并提供封闭式数值或代数答案"] --> B["博士科学家撰写需三至五小时的原创研究子任务，基于活跃研究领域"]
    B["博士科学家撰写需三至五小时的原创研究子任务，基于活跃研究领域"] --> C["独立领域专家审查每个问题、答案和解答的正确性及指南符合性"]
    C["独立领域专家审查每个问题、答案和解答的正确性及指南符合性"] --> D["撰写者与审查者的分歧通过共识解决或舍弃问题"]
    D["撰写者与审查者的分歧通过共识解决或舍弃问题"] --> E["元审查从500余道奥赛和200余道研究问题中筛选出100和60道黄金集"]
    E["元审查从500余道奥赛和200余道研究问题中筛选出100和60道黄金集"] --> F["研究问题配10分评分标准，评估中间推理步骤和最终答案正确性"]
    F["研究问题配10分评分标准，评估中间推理步骤和最终答案正确性"] --> G["奥赛答案通过表达式等价或模糊字符串匹配在20次独立试验中评分"]
    G["奥赛答案通过表达式等价或模糊字符串匹配在20次独立试验中评分"] --> H["研究答案由GPT-5模型分配评分标准分数，30次试验平均并设7分阈值"]

name: GAIA
intro: GAIA是一个为通用AI助手设计的基准测试，旨在评估AI在现实世界问题中的表现。这些问题需要推理、多模态处理、网页浏览和工具使用等基本能力。GAIA的问题对人类来说概念简单，但对最先进的AI来说极具挑战性，旨在推动通用人工智能（AGI）的发展。
build_method: 人工标注
eval_feature: 思维链, 零样本, 多模态, 工具使用, 网页浏览
eval_feature_en: Chain-of-Thought, Zero-shot, Multimodal, Tool Use, Web Browsing
intro_en: GAIA is a benchmark designed for general AI assistants to evaluate their performance on real-world problems. These problems require core abilities such as reasoning, multimodal processing, web browsing, and tool use. GAIA’s tasks are conceptually simple for humans but highly challenging for state-of-the-art AI, aiming to advance the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI).
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Problem Identification]
    B --> C[Task Formulation]
    C --> D{{Manual Annotation}}
    D --> E[Multimodal Data Integration]
    E --> F[Tool Use Scenario Design]
    F --> G{Task Complexity Check}
    G -->|Pass| H[Human Expert Review]
    G -->|Fail| C
    H --> I(GAIA Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[AI Assistant Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Craft 466 real-world questions conceptually simple for humans but requiring complex action sequences"] --> B["Ensure each question requires reasoning, multi-modality handling, web browsing, or tool-use proficiency"]
    B["Ensure each question requires reasoning, multi-modality handling, web browsing, or tool-use proficiency"] --> C["Design questions to admit unique, factual answers enabling simple and robust automatic evaluation"]
    C["Design questions to admit unique, factual answers enabling simple and robust automatic evaluation"] --> D["Validate questions with human respondents to establish 92% human performance baseline"]
    D["Validate questions with human respondents to establish 92% human performance baseline"] --> E["Release all 466 questions publicly while retaining 300 answers for leaderboard evaluation"]
    E["Release all 466 questions publicly while retaining 300 answers for leaderboard evaluation"] --> F["Evaluate AI systems using exact match against ground truth answers on the leaderboard"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["设计466个对人类概念简单但需复杂操作序列的真实问题"] --> B["确保每个问题需要推理、多模态、网页浏览或工具使用能力"]
    B["确保每个问题需要推理、多模态、网页浏览或工具使用能力"] --> C["设计问题使其具有唯一事实答案以支持简单自动评估"]
    C["设计问题使其具有唯一事实答案以支持简单自动评估"] --> D["通过人类受试者验证问题并建立92%的人类基线"]
    D["通过人类受试者验证问题并建立92%的人类基线"] --> E["公开发布全部466个问题但保留300个答案用于排行榜"]
    E["公开发布全部466个问题但保留300个答案用于排行榜"] --> F["在排行榜上用精确匹配评估AI系统与标准答案"]

name: Gaia2
intro: Gaia2是一个用于在真实、异步环境中评估大型语言模型代理的基准。它引入了独立于代理行为而演变的环境场景，要求代理在时间限制下操作、适应动态和嘈杂的事件、解决模糊性并与其他代理协作。每个场景都配有写操作验证器，支持细粒度的动作级评估。该基准建立在开源的ARE平台之上，旨在推动下一代实用代理系统的发展。
build_method: 人工构建与合成数据
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
task_type: Agent评估, 复杂推理, 动态适应, 多步推理
eval_feature: 异步, 动态环境, 多Agent协作, 时间限制, 噪声鲁棒性, 模糊性解决
task_type_en: Agent Evaluation, Complex Reasoning, Dynamic Adaptation, Multi-step Reasoning
eval_feature_en: Asynchronous, dynamic environment, multi-agent collaboration, time constraints, noise robustness, ambiguity resolution
intro_en: Gaia2 is a benchmark for evaluating large language model agents in real, asynchronous environments. It introduces environment scenarios that evolve independently of agent behavior, requiring agents to operate within time constraints, adapt to dynamic and noisy events, resolve ambiguities, and collaborate with other agents. Each scenario includes a write operation validator, enabling fine-grained, action-level evaluation. Built on the open-source ARE platform, this benchmark aims to advance the development of next-generation practical agent systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[定义基准目标] --> B[构建Agents Research Environments (ARE) 平台]
    B --> C[设计移动环境 (Smartphone-like)]
    C --> D[选择并集成应用和工具 (e.g., Email, Messaging, Calendar)]
    D --> E(合成数据生成)
    E --> F(PersonaHub人物采样)
    F --> G[跨应用数据一致性传播]
    G --> H[Human Annotation场景 (1120个)]
    H --> I[创建Gaia2场景集 (800个独特可Validation场景)]
    I --> J[创建Gaia2-mini子集 (160个场景)]
    J --> K[场景增强 (额外320个场景)]
    K --> L[开发写操作Validation器 (ARE Verifier)]
    L --> M[定义Evaluation指标 (细粒度、动作级别Evaluation)]
    M --> N[基于ReAct循环的代理编排]
    N --> O[执行代理Evaluation]
    O --> P[Validation器检查状态变化动作] 
    P --> Q{Validation结果}
    Q -- 通过 --> R[Final Dataset组成]
    Q -- 失败 --> H
    R --> S[发布Gaia2基准]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Create 1,120 human-annotated scenarios in smartphone-like environment with realistic apps"] --> B["Design scenarios motivated by real use cases: information seeking, scheduled tasks, and multi-agent collaboration"]
    B["Design scenarios motivated by real use cases: information seeking, scheduled tasks, and multi-agent collaboration"] --> C["Build asynchronous environments on ARE platform where state evolves independently of agent actions"]
    C["Build asynchronous environments on ARE platform where state evolves independently of agent actions"] --> D["Pair each scenario with write-action verifier to check state-changing actions against oracle annotations"]
    D["Pair each scenario with write-action verifier to check state-changing actions against oracle annotations"] --> E["Equip agents with ReAct-like scaffold for interaction with dynamic and noisy events"]
    E["Equip agents with ReAct-like scaffold for interaction with dynamic and noisy events"] --> F["Run agents under temporal constraints and budget limits across all scenarios"]
    F["Run agents under temporal constraints and budget limits across all scenarios"] --> G["Evaluate using pass@1 metric with action-level verification from write-action verifiers"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["在类智能机环境中创建1120个人工标注场景"] --> B["设计真实用例驱动的场景：信息检索、定时任务和多智能体协作"]
    B["设计真实用例驱动的场景：信息检索、定时任务和多智能体协作"] --> C["在ARE平台构建状态独立演化的异步环境"]
    C["在ARE平台构建状态独立演化的异步环境"] --> D["为每个场景配对写操作验证器以检查状态变化动作"]
    D["为每个场景配对写操作验证器以检查状态变化动作"] --> E["为智能体配备类ReAct脚手架以交互动态噪声事件"]
    E["为智能体配备类ReAct脚手架以交互动态噪声事件"] --> F["在时间和预算约束下运行智能体完成所有场景"]
    F["在时间和预算约束下运行智能体完成所有场景"] --> G["使用pass@1指标和动作级验证器评估"]

name: GaokaoBench
intro: GaokaoBench是一个直观的评测基准，它使用2010至2022年的中国高考题目来评估大型语言模型。该基准包含1781道客观题和1030道主观题，覆盖了理科和文科的9个科目。它采用零样本设置和人工评分相结合的方式，全面考察模型的语言理解、逻辑推理和生成能力。
build_method: 基于2010-2022年中国高考试题，通过自动化脚本和人工标注结合的方式构建
metric: Scoring Rate, Converted Total Score, Spearman Correlation, Kendall-Tau Correlation
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答, 填空题, 改错题
task_type_en: Multiple Choice, Open-ended QA, Fill-in-the-blank, Error correction
build_method_en: Constructed based on 2010-2022 China Gaokao exam questions, combining automated scripts and manual annotation
intro_en: GaokaoBench is an intuitive evaluation benchmark that uses Chinese Gaokao exam questions from 2010 to 2022 to assess large language models. The benchmark includes 1,781 objective questions and 1,030 subjective questions, covering nine science and humanities subjects. It employs a zero-shot setting combined with human scoring to comprehensively evaluate models' language understanding, logical reasoning, and generation abilities.

    H -- 主观题 --> J[1030道主观题]
    I --> K[客观题Evaluation (正则匹配)]
    J --> L[主观题人工评分 (两位教师平均分)]
    J --> M[主观题LLM评分 (GPT-4-turbo)]
    L --> N{评分一致性检查}
    M --> N
    N -- 通过 --> O[Final Dataset构成]
    N -- 失败 --> J
    O --> P[零样本设置下的LLMEvaluation]
    P --> Q[LLM性能分析]
    Q --> R[GAOKAO-BenchEvaluation结果]

name: GaRAGe
intro: GaRAGe是一个大型RAG基准，包含人工整理的长篇答案及每个依据段落的注释，支持细粒度评估LLM在生成RAG答案时识别相关依据的能力。该基准涵盖2366个多样化问题，包含3.5万多条来自私有文档和网络的注释段落，反映真实RAG应用场景。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
metric: Relevance-Aware Factuality Score, F1 in attribution, true positive rate in deflections
task_type: 开放式问答, 事实验证
intro_en: GaRAGe is a large-scale RAG benchmark featuring manually curated long-form answers and annotated supporting paragraphs for each, enabling fine-grained evaluation of LLMs' ability to identify relevant evidence when generating RAG answers. The benchmark includes 2,366 diverse questions and over 35,000 annotated paragraphs from private documents and the web, reflecting real-world RAG application scenarios.
metric_en: Relevance-Aware Factuality Score, F1 in attribution, true positive rate in deflections
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR

    C["Generate complex questions by blending multi-source snippets using exemplar prompts"] --> D["Filter questions using wellformedness classifier, named entity check, and SentenceTransformer deduplication"]
    D["Filter questions using wellformedness classifier, named entity check, and SentenceTransformer deduplication"] --> E["Decompose questions into sub-queries and retrieve grounding from Web and private knowledge bases"]
    E["Decompose questions into sub-queries and retrieve grounding from Web and private knowledge bases"] --> F["Professional annotators validate questions and annotate relevancy of each grounding passage"]
    F["Professional annotators validate questions and annotate relevancy of each grounding passage"] --> G["Annotators curate comprehensive long-form answers with attribution markers to relevant grounding sources"]

flowchart LR
    A["提示LLM从种子主题生成网络搜索计划和目标查询"] --> B["对每个生成的查询执行网络搜索收集信息片段"]
    B["对每个生成的查询执行网络搜索收集信息片段"] --> C["使用示例提示混合多源片段生成复杂问题"]

    D["用完整性分类器、命名实体检查和去重过滤问题"] --> E["将问题分解为子查询并从网络和私有知识库检索依据"]
    E["将问题分解为子查询并从网络和私有知识库检索依据"] --> F["专业标注员验证问题并标注每个依据段落的相关性"]
    F["专业标注员验证问题并标注每个依据段落的相关性"] --> G["标注员编写包含相关依据归因标记的综合长篇答案"]

name: GDPVal
intro: GDPval是一个评估AI模型在真实世界中具有经济价值任务表现的基准测试。它涵盖了美国劳工统计局针对44个职业的大部分工作活动，这些职业来自对美国GDP贡献最大的9个行业。任务是根据平均拥有14年经验的行业专业人士的代表性工作构建的。该基准测试还提供了一个包含220个任务的黄金子集和公共自动化评分服务。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: GDPVal is a benchmark for evaluating AI models' performance on economically valuable tasks in the real world. It covers most work activities for 44 occupations from the 9 industries contributing most to the US GDP, based on data from the US Bureau of Labor Statistics. Tasks are constructed to represent work performed by industry professionals with an average of 14 years of experience. The benchmark also provides a gold subset of 220 tasks and a public automated scoring service.
scale_en: 44 items professions, 220 items golden subset tasks
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select 9 sectors contributing over 5% to US GDP from Federal Reserve Q2 2024 data"] --> B["Identify 5 highest-wage predominantly digital occupations per sector using O*NET and BLS wage data"]
    B["Identify 5 highest-wage predominantly digital occupations per sector using O*NET and BLS wage data"] --> C["Recruit industry experts with 4+ years experience through interviews, background checks, training, and quizzes"]
    C["Recruit industry experts with 4+ years experience through interviews, background checks, training, and quizzes"] --> D["Experts create tasks from actual work product and classify against O*NET occupational task categories"]
    D["Experts create tasks from actual work product and classify against O*NET occupational task categories"] --> E["Review each task through iterative pipeline with minimum 3 human expert reviews and revisions"]
    E["Review each task through iterative pipeline with minimum 3 human expert reviews and revisions"] --> F["Grade 220 gold subset tasks using blinded expert pairwise comparisons of model versus human deliverables"]
    F["Grade 220 gold subset tasks using blinded expert pairwise comparisons of model versus human deliverables"] --> G["Train experimental automated grader on expert comparisons achieving 66% agreement with human graders"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从美联储2024年第二季度数据中选择贡献超过5%的9个行业"] --> B["使用O*NET和BLS工资数据识别每个行业中5个最高薪数字化职业"]
    B["使用O*NET和BLS工资数据识别每个行业中5个最高薪数字化职业"] --> C["通过面试、背景调查、培训和测验招募4年以上经验的行业专家"]
    C["通过面试、背景调查、培训和测验招募4年以上经验的行业专家"] --> D["专家基于实际工作产品创建任务并对照O*NET职业任务分类"]
    D["专家基于实际工作产品创建任务并对照O*NET职业任务分类"] --> E["通过迭代流程审查每个任务至少经过3轮专家评审和修订"]
    E["通过迭代流程审查每个任务至少经过3轮专家评审和修订"] --> F["使用盲测专家配对比较对220个黄金子集任务评分"]
    F["使用盲测专家配对比较对220个黄金子集任务评分"] --> G["训练实验性自动评分器在专家比较上达到66%的人类一致性"]

name: GEdit-Bench
intro: GEdit-Bench是一个基于真实世界用户指令的图像编辑评测基准。它旨在提供一个更真实、更全面的评估框架，以衡量图像编辑模型的性能。该基准的发布旨在缩小开源模型与领先的闭源模型（如GPT-4o和Gemini）在图像编辑能力上的差距。
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
intro_en: GEdit-Bench is an image editing evaluation benchmark based on real-world user instructions. It aims to provide a more realistic and comprehensive framework for assessing the performance of image editing models. The benchmark is released to narrow the gap in image editing capabilities between open-source models and leading closed-source models such as GPT-4o and Gemini.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Carefully collect images and editing prompts from real-world user instructions"] --> B["Ensure collected data covers diverse editing requirements and prompt variations"]
    B["Ensure collected data covers diverse editing requirements and prompt variations"] --> C["Curate benchmark samples to reflect practical user-grounded editing scenarios"]
    C["Curate benchmark samples to reflect practical user-grounded editing scenarios"] --> D["Evaluate editing models on GEdit-Bench using the curated image-instruction pairs"]
    D["Evaluate editing models on GEdit-Bench using the curated image-instruction pairs"] --> E["Compare open-source and proprietary model performance on real-world editing tasks"]
    E["Compare open-source and proprietary model performance on real-world editing tasks"] --> F["Measure edit accuracy, generalization, and generated image quality across models"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从真实用户指令中精心收集图像和编辑提示"] --> B["确保收集数据涵盖多样化编辑需求和提示变体"]
    B["确保收集数据涵盖多样化编辑需求和提示变体"] --> C["策划基准样本以反映实际用户编辑场景"]
    C["策划基准样本以反映实际用户编辑场景"] --> D["使用策划的图像指令对在GEdit-Bench上评估编辑模型"]
    D["使用策划的图像指令对在GEdit-Bench上评估编辑模型"] --> E["比较开源和专有模型在真实编辑任务上的性能"]
    E["比较开源和专有模型在真实编辑任务上的性能"] --> F["测量各模型的编辑准确性、泛化性和生成图像质量"]

name: GENIUS
intro: GENIUS（Generative Fluid Intelligence Evaluation Suite）是一个用于评估统一多模态模型生成式流体智能的基准，重点考察模型在即时上下文中归纳模式、执行临时约束和适应情境知识的能力。
org: Peking University, CUHK, StepFun, PolyU, MSRA
build_method: 人工构建, 专家构建
metric: Rule Compliance, Visual Consistency, Aesthetic Quality
eval_feature: 归纳推理, 约束遵循, 情境适应
intro_en: GENIUS is a benchmark for evaluating generative fluid intelligence in unified multimodal models. It measures how models induce implicit patterns, execute ad-hoc constraints, and adapt to contextual knowledge in image generation tasks.
build_method_en: Expert Curation, Interleaved Multimodal Context Design
metric_en: Rule Compliance, Visual Consistency, Aesthetic Quality
eval_feature_en: Inductive Inference, Constraint Following, Contextual Adaptation
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["先定义 GFI 的三类原语"] --> B["隐式模式归纳 / 临时约束执行 / 情境知识适应"]
    B --> C["围绕三类原语组织 3 个维度、5 个任务、20 个子任务"]
    C --> D["为每个样本设计图文交错上下文"]
    D --> E["确认移除任一模态后任务都会失效"]
    E --> F["让统一多模态模型生成图像结果"]
    F --> G["为样本补充人工整理的 eval hint"]
    G --> H["用 Gemini-3-Pro 做结构化判分"]
    H --> I["汇总 Rule Compliance、Visual Consistency、Aesthetic Quality"]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Define the three primitives of GFI first"] --> B["Implicit pattern induction / ad-hoc constraint execution / contextual adaptation"]
    B --> C["Organize 3 dimensions, 5 tasks, and 20 sub-tasks around them"]
    C --> D["Create interleaved image-text contexts for every sample"]
    D --> E["Confirm that removing any modality makes the task fail"]
    E --> F["Ask unified multimodal models to generate the output image"]
    F --> G["Attach manually curated evaluation hints to each sample"]
    G --> H["Run structured judging with Gemini-3-Pro"]
    H --> I["Aggregate Rule Compliance, Visual Consistency, and Aesthetic Quality"]

name: GMAI-MMBench
intro: GMAI-MMBench是一个全面的通用医疗AI基准，旨在评估大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在各种医疗应用中的有效性。该基准解决了现有基准在临床相关性、评估不完整性和交互式LVLMs指导不足等方面的局限性。它由284个数据集构建，涵盖38种医学图像模态、18项临床相关任务、18个科室和4种感知粒度，采用视觉问答（VQA）形式。
org: Shanghai AI Laboratory, University of Washington, Monash University, East China Normal University, U
intro_en: GMAI-MMBench is a comprehensive general medical AI benchmark designed to evaluate the effectiveness of large vision-language models (LVLMs) across various medical applications. It addresses limitations of existing benchmarks in clinical relevance, incomplete evaluation, and insufficient guidance for interactive LVLMs. The benchmark is built from 284 datasets, covering 38 medical image modalities, 18 clinically relevant tasks, 18 departments, and 4 levels of perceptual granularity, using a visual question answering (VQA) format.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect 284 datasets from Internet searches and hospital collaborations covering classification, detection, and segmentation"] --> B["Standardize images to 2D RGB using SA-Med2D-20M protocol and labels using MeSH vocabulary"]
    B["Standardize images to 2D RGB using SA-Med2D-20M protocol and labels using MeSH vocabulary"] --> C["Categorize labels into lexical tree covering 18 tasks, 18 departments, and 4 perceptual granularities"]
    C["Categorize labels into lexical tree covering 18 tasks, 18 departments, and 4 perceptual granularities"] --> D["Generate question-answering pairs in VQA format through a reliable pipeline"]
    D["Generate question-answering pairs in VQA format through a reliable pipeline"] --> E["Manually validate generated question-answering pairs for quality assurance"]
    E["Manually validate generated question-answering pairs for quality assurance"] --> F["Select approximately 26K questions with varying perceptual granularities from validated cases"]
    F["Select approximately 26K questions with varying perceptual granularities from validated cases"] --> G["Evaluate 50 LVLMs in zero-shot setting using VLMEvalKit and Multi-Modality-Arena frameworks"]
    G["Evaluate 50 LVLMs in zero-shot setting using VLMEvalKit and Multi-Modality-Arena frameworks"] --> H["Calculate macro-averaged accuracy for single-choice and accuracy-recall for multiple-choice questions"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从互联网和医院收集284个数据集涵盖分类检测分割任务"] --> B["用SA-Med2D-20M协议标准化图像用MeSH标准化标签"]
    B["用SA-Med2D-20M协议标准化图像用MeSH标准化标签"] --> C["将标签分类为词汇树涵盖18任务18科室4感知粒度"]
    C["将标签分类为词汇树涵盖18任务18科室4感知粒度"] --> D["通过可靠流程生成VQA格式的问答对"]
    D["通过可靠流程生成VQA格式的问答对"] --> E["人工验证生成的问答对以确保质量"]
    E["人工验证生成的问答对以确保质量"] --> F["从验证案例中选择约26K个不同感知粒度的问题"]
    F["从验证案例中选择约26K个不同感知粒度的问题"] --> G["用VLMEvalKit和Multi-Modality-Arena零样本评估50个模型"]
    G["用VLMEvalKit和Multi-Modality-Arena零样本评估50个模型"] --> H["计算单选题宏平均准确率和多选题准确率召回率"]

name: GPQA
intro: GPQA包含448个由领域专家编写的生物、物理和化学多项选择题，设计为“谷歌难题”，难以被熟练非专家和先进AI系统解答。旨在促进可扩展监督研究，帮助专家从超越人类能力的AI系统中获得真实信息。
org: New York University, Google DeepMind, Cohere
build_method: 专家构建
eval_feature: 领域特定, 防污染
intro_en: GPQA contains 448 multiple-choice questions in biology, physics, and chemistry, written by domain experts and designed as "Google-proof" problems that are difficult for skilled non-experts and advanced AI systems to solve. It aims to promote scalable supervision research and help experts obtain reliable information from AI systems that surpass human capabilities.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Solicit graduate-level multiple-choice questions from PhD experts in biology, physics, and chemistry subdomains"] --> B["Validate question correctness and quality with domain experts holding or pursuing PhDs"]
    B["Validate question correctness and quality with domain experts holding or pursuing PhDs"] --> C["Test questions with highly skilled non-expert validators who are PhDs in other domains"]
    C["Test questions with highly skilled non-expert validators who are PhDs in other domains"] --> D["Allow non-experts unrestricted web access excluding LLM assistants, averaging 37 minutes per question"]
    D["Allow non-experts unrestricted web access excluding LLM assistants, averaging 37 minutes per question"] --> E["Confirm questions are Google-proof by verifying non-experts achieve only 34% accuracy"]
    E["Confirm questions are Google-proof by verifying non-experts achieve only 34% accuracy"] --> F["Collect expert retrospective feedback to identify and discount clear mistakes in their answers"]
    F["Collect expert retrospective feedback to identify and discount clear mistakes in their answers"] --> G["Evaluate AI systems using GPT-4 based baselines on the finalized 448-question dataset"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["向生物、物理和化学领域博士专家征集研究生级多项选择题"] --> B["由领域内博士专家验证问题的正确性和质量"]
    B["由领域内博士专家验证问题的正确性和质量"] --> C["由其他领域博士组成的非专家验证者测试问题"]
    C["由其他领域博士组成的非专家验证者测试问题"] --> D["允许非专家无限制使用网络但不含LLM助手，平均每题37分钟"]
    D["允许非专家无限制使用网络但不含LLM助手，平均每题37分钟"] --> E["通过非专家仅达34%准确率确认问题为谷歌难题"]
    E["通过非专家仅达34%准确率确认问题为谷歌难题"] --> F["收集专家回顾反馈以识别和排除其答案中的明显错误"]
    F["收集专家回顾反馈以识别和排除其答案中的明显错误"] --> G["使用基于GPT-4的基线在最终448题数据集上评估AI系统"]

name: GPQA Diamond
intro: GPQA Diamond是GPQA (Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A) 基准的一个精选子集，包含198个由领域专家验证的最高质量问题。这些问题涵盖物理、化学和生物学等研究生级别的科学领域，被广泛用于评估大型语言模型的高级科学推理能力。
build_method: 专家构建
eval_feature: 领域特定, 防污染
intro_en: GPQA Diamond is a curated subset of the GPQA (Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A) benchmark, containing 198 highest-quality questions verified by domain experts. These questions cover graduate-level scientific fields such as physics, chemistry, and biology, and are widely used to evaluate the advanced scientific reasoning abilities of large language models.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Solicit graduate-level multiple-choice questions from PhD experts in biology, physics, and chemistry subdomains"] --> B["Validate question correctness and difficulty with domain experts holding or pursuing PhDs in corresponding fields"]
    B["Validate question correctness and difficulty with domain experts holding or pursuing PhDs in corresponding fields"] --> C["Test questions with highly skilled non-expert validators who are PhDs in other domains"]
    C["Test questions with highly skilled non-expert validators who are PhDs in other domains"] --> D["Allow non-experts unrestricted web access for over 30 minutes per question, excluding LLM assistants"]
    D["Allow non-experts unrestricted web access for over 30 minutes per question, excluding LLM assistants"] --> E["Confirm questions are Google-proof by verifying non-expert accuracy remains around 34% despite web access"]
    E["Confirm questions are Google-proof by verifying non-expert accuracy remains around 34% despite web access"] --> F["Identify and discount clear retrospective mistakes from expert validation to calculate adjusted accuracy"]
    F["Identify and discount clear retrospective mistakes from expert validation to calculate adjusted accuracy"] --> G["Curate Diamond subset of 198 highest-quality questions validated through the expert and non-expert process"]
    G["Curate Diamond subset of 198 highest-quality questions validated through the expert and non-expert process"] --> H["Evaluate AI systems using multiple-choice accuracy on the validated graduate-level scientific questions"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["向生物、物理和化学领域的博士专家征集研究生级别选择题"] --> B["由相应领域的博士专家验证问题的正确性和难度"]
    B["由相应领域的博士专家验证问题的正确性和难度"] --> C["由其他领域的博士作为非专家验证者测试问题"]
    C["由其他领域的博士作为非专家验证者测试问题"] --> D["允许非专家每题使用网络超30分钟但不可用大模型"]
    D["允许非专家每题使用网络超30分钟但不可用大模型"] --> E["确认非专家即使使用网络准确率仅34%以验证防搜索性"]
    E["确认非专家即使使用网络准确率仅34%以验证防搜索性"] --> F["识别并排除专家事后发现的明显错误以计算调整准确率"]
    F["识别并排除专家事后发现的明显错误以计算调整准确率"] --> G["筛选出经专家和非专家流程验证的198道最高质量问题"]
    G["筛选出经专家和非专家流程验证的198道最高质量问题"] --> H["用多选题准确率评估AI系统在验证过的研究生级科学问题上的表现"]

name: GraphWalks
intro: GraphWalks是一个多跳推理长上下文基准测试。在该基准测试中，模型会获得一个以边列表表示的图，并被要求执行特定操作。模型需要根据图的结构和操作描述，返回操作结果中的节点集合，例如执行广度优先搜索（BFS）或查找节点的父节点。
intro_en: GraphWalks is a multi-hop reasoning long-context benchmark. In this benchmark, the model is given a graph represented by an edge list and is required to perform specific operations. The model must return the set of nodes resulting from the operation based on the graph structure and operation description, such as performing breadth-first search (BFS) or finding a node's parent.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[GraphWalks Benchmark Construction] --> B[Data Generation Process]
    B --> C[Graph Structure Generation]
    C --> D[Operation Type Selection]
    D{Operation Type} -- BFS --> E[BFS Operation Definition]
    D -- Parents --> F[Parents Operation Definition]
    E --> G[Ground Truth Answer Generation (BFS)]
    F --> H[Ground Truth Answer Generation (Parents)]
    G --> I(Raw Data Samples)
    H --> I
    I --> J[Prompt Formulation]
    J --> K(Prompt Data)
    K --> L(Answer Data)
    L --> M(Metadata)
    M --> N[Initial Dataset Compilation]
    N --> O{Quality Control & Validation}
    O -- Bug Identified --> P[Ground Truth Correction]
    P --> N
    O -- No Bug --> Q[Final Dataset Composition]
    Q --> R[Evaluation Methodology Definition]
    R --> S[Answer Extraction Logic]
    S --> T[Metric Calculation (Recall, Precision, F1)]
    T --> U(Evaluation Results)
    Q --> V(GraphWalks Dataset)

name: GSM8K
intro: GSM8K是一个包含8500个高质量、语言多样的小学数学应用题的数据集，旨在诊断和评估大型语言模型在多步数学推理方面的能力。尽管这些问题在概念上相对简单，但即便是最大规模的Transformer模型也难以在该基准上取得高分。
eval_feature: 思维链
intro_en: GSM8K is a dataset of 8,500 high-quality, linguistically diverse elementary math word problems designed to diagnose and evaluate large language models' abilities in multi-step mathematical reasoning. Although these problems are conceptually simple, even the largest Transformer models struggle to achieve high scores on this benchmark.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Recruit human problem writers to create grade school math word problems"] --> B["Design problems requiring 2 to 8 steps using basic arithmetic operations"]
    B["Design problems requiring 2 to 8 steps using basic arithmetic operations"] --> C["Ensure high linguistic diversity by avoiding templated or superficially similar problems"]
    C["Ensure high linguistic diversity by avoiding templated or superficially similar problems"] --> D["Perform quality control based on workers' answer agreement to reduce errors"]
    D["Perform quality control based on workers' answer agreement to reduce errors"] --> E["Segment 8.5K problems into 7.5K training and 1K test sets"]
    E["Segment 8.5K problems into 7.5K training and 1K test sets"] --> F["Generate multiple candidate solutions for each test problem using language models"]
    F["Generate multiple candidate solutions for each test problem using language models"] --> G["Train verifiers to judge correctness of model-generated solutions"]
    G["Train verifiers to judge correctness of model-generated solutions"] --> H["Select the solution ranked highest by the verifier as final answer"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["招募人工问题编写者创建小学数学应用题"] --> B["设计需要2到8步基本算术运算的问题"]
    B["设计需要2到8步基本算术运算的问题"] --> C["避免模板化或表面相似问题以确保语言多样性"]
    C["避免模板化或表面相似问题以确保语言多样性"] --> D["基于工作者答案一致性进行质量控制以减少错误"]
    D["基于工作者答案一致性进行质量控制以减少错误"] --> E["将8.5K问题分为7.5K训练集和1K测试集"]
    E["将8.5K问题分为7.5K训练集和1K测试集"] --> F["用语言模型为每个测试问题生成多个候选解"]
    F["用语言模型为每个测试问题生成多个候选解"] --> G["训练验证器判断模型生成解的正确性"]
    G["训练验证器判断模型生成解的正确性"] --> H["选择验证器排名最高的解作为最终答案"]

name: HalluEval
intro: HalluEval是一个用于评估大型语言模型幻觉的大规模基准。它包含约35000个带有人工标注的生成样本，涵盖了通用、问答、对话和摘要等多种场景。该基准旨在全面评估和分析大型语言模型在识别和避免生成不真实或与来源不符内容方面的能力。
org: Renmin University of China, Université de Montréal, Hugging Face
build_method: LLM生成, 人工标注
intro_en: HalluEval is a large-scale benchmark for evaluating hallucinations in large language models. It contains approximately 35,000 human-annotated generated samples across various scenarios, including general tasks, QA, dialogue, and summarization. The benchmark aims to comprehensively assess and analyze large language models' ability to identify and avoid generating false or source-inconsistent content.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Sample Alpaca's 52K instruction dataset as general user queries"] --> B["Generate three ChatGPT responses per query and retain 5,000 with lowest similarity"]
    B["Generate three ChatGPT responses per query and retain 5,000 with lowest similarity"] --> C["Use ChatGPT with conversational instructions to generate diverse hallucinated candidate answers"]
    C["Use ChatGPT with conversational instructions to generate diverse hallucinated candidate answers"] --> D["Apply max-voting filtering to select high-quality hallucinated samples from candidates"]
    D["Apply max-voting filtering to select high-quality hallucinated samples from candidates"] --> E["Hire human labelers to annotate whether responses contain hallucinations and mark spans"]
    E["Hire human labelers to annotate whether responses contain hallucinations and mark spans"] --> F["Collect 30,000 task-specific examples from QA, dialogue, and summarization tasks"]
    F["Collect 30,000 task-specific examples from QA, dialogue, and summarization tasks"] --> G["Evaluate LLMs on recognizing hallucinations with and without external knowledge or reasoning"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从Alpaca的52K指令数据集中采样通用用户查询"] --> B["为每个查询生成三个ChatGPT响应并保留相似度最低的5000个"]
    B["为每个查询生成三个ChatGPT响应并保留相似度最低的5000个"] --> C["使用ChatGPT和对话指令生成多样化的幻觉候选答案"]
    C["使用ChatGPT和对话指令生成多样化的幻觉候选答案"] --> D["应用最大投票过滤从候选中选出高质量幻觉样本"]
    D["应用最大投票过滤从候选中选出高质量幻觉样本"] --> E["雇佣人工标注员标注响应是否含幻觉并标记片段"]
    E["雇佣人工标注员标注响应是否含幻觉并标记片段"] --> F["从问答、对话和摘要任务收集30000个特定任务样本"]
    F["从问答、对话和摘要任务收集30000个特定任务样本"] --> G["评估LLM在有无外部知识或推理下识别幻觉的能力"]

name: HalluLens
intro: HalluLens是一个用于评估大型语言模型幻觉的综合基准。它提出了一个清晰的幻觉分类法，区分了与训练数据不一致的外源性幻觉和与输入上下文不一致的内源性幻觉。该基准包含动态生成的测试集以防止数据泄漏，并引入了新的外源性幻觉任务，旨在为模型的事实性和一致性评估提供标准化框架。
org: FAIR at Meta, GenAI at Meta, HKUST
build_method: 动态生成
metric: Hallucinated when not refused, Correct answer rate, False acceptance rate
task_type: 开放式问答, 生成, 事实验证, 分类, 安全测试
eval_feature: 动态, 实时, 防污染, 对抗性
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Generation, Fact Verification, Classification, Safety Testing
intro_en: HalluLens is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating hallucinations in large language models. It proposes a clear hallucination taxonomy, distinguishing exogenous hallucinations inconsistent with training data from endogenous hallucinations inconsistent with input context. The benchmark includes dynamically generated test sets to prevent data leakage and introduces new exogenous hallucination tasks, aiming to provide a standardized framework for assessing model factuality and consistency.
metric_en: Hallucinated when not refused, Correct answer rate, False acceptance rate
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Establish hallucination taxonomy distinguishing extrinsic from intrinsic types based on reference sources"] --> B["Construct PreciseWikiQA task using Wikipedia to evaluate precise short answer consistency with training data"]
    B["Construct PreciseWikiQA task using Wikipedia to evaluate precise short answer consistency with training data"] --> C["Construct LongWiki task to assess consistency in detailed long-form content generation"]
    C["Construct LongWiki task to assess consistency in detailed long-form content generation"] --> D["Construct NonExistentRefusal task with unanswerable questions to test knowledge boundary recognition"]
    D["Construct NonExistentRefusal task with unanswerable questions to test knowledge boundary recognition"] --> E["Integrate existing intrinsic hallucination tasks evaluating consistency with input context"]
    E["Integrate existing intrinsic hallucination tasks evaluating consistency with input context"] --> F["Implement dynamic test set generation mechanism to prevent data leakage and benchmark saturation"]
    F["Implement dynamic test set generation mechanism to prevent data leakage and benchmark saturation"] --> G["Evaluate LLMs on extrinsic and intrinsic tasks measuring hallucination across different sources"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["建立幻觉分类法区分外源性和内源性类型"] --> B["构建PreciseWikiQA任务评估简短答案一致性"]
    B["构建PreciseWikiQA任务评估简短答案一致性"] --> C["构建LongWiki任务评估长文本内容一致性"]
    C["构建LongWiki任务评估长文本内容一致性"] --> D["构建NonExistentRefusal任务测试知识边界识别"]
    D["构建NonExistentRefusal任务测试知识边界识别"] --> E["整合现有内源性幻觉任务评估输入一致性"]
    E["整合现有内源性幻觉任务评估输入一致性"] --> F["实施动态测试集生成机制防止数据泄漏"]
    F["实施动态测试集生成机制防止数据泄漏"] --> G["在外源性和内源性任务上评估模型幻觉"]

name: HalluQA
intro: HalluQA是针对中文大型语言模型幻觉现象的问答基准。它包含450个跨越多个领域的精心设计的对抗性问题，特别考虑了中国的历史文化、风俗习惯和社会现象。该基准在构建过程中区分了模仿性虚假和事实性错误两种幻觉类型，并基于GLM-130B和ChatGPT构建了对抗性样本。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建, LLM生成
task_type: 开放式问答, 事实验证, 选择题
eval_feature: 对抗性, 领域特定
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Fact Verification, Multiple Choice
build_method_en: Human Annotation, Expert Curation, LLM Generation
intro_en: HalluQA is a question-answering benchmark targeting hallucination phenomena in Chinese large language models. It contains 450 carefully designed adversarial questions across multiple domains, with special consideration of Chinese history, culture, customs, and social phenomena. The benchmark distinguishes between imitative falsehoods and factual errors as two types of hallucinations and constructs adversarial samples based on GLM-130B and ChatGPT.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Design 450 adversarial questions spanning multiple domains with Chinese cultural specificity"] --> B["Categorize questions into imitative falsehoods and factual errors hallucination types"]
    B["Categorize questions into imitative falsehoods and factual errors hallucination types"] --> C["Construct adversarial samples using GLM-130B and ChatGPT as base models"]
    C["Construct adversarial samples using GLM-130B and ChatGPT as base models"] --> D["Collect responses from 24 Chinese large language models on the questions"]
    D["Collect responses from 24 Chinese large language models on the questions"] --> E["Use GPT-4 as automated judge to determine if model outputs are hallucinated"]
    E["Use GPT-4 as automated judge to determine if model outputs are hallucinated"] --> F["Calculate non-hallucination rates for each model across question categories"]
    F["Calculate non-hallucination rates for each model across question categories"] --> G["Analyze primary hallucination types and causes for different model categories"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["设计450个跨领域对抗性问题,涵盖中国文化特色"] --> B["将问题分为模仿性虚假和事实性错误两类"]
    B["将问题分为模仿性虚假和事实性错误两类"] --> C["基于GLM-130B和ChatGPT构建对抗性样本"]
    C["基于GLM-130B和ChatGPT构建对抗性样本"] --> D["收集24个中文大模型对问题的回答"]
    D["收集24个中文大模型对问题的回答"] --> E["使用GPT-4自动判断模型输出是否存在幻觉"]
    E["使用GPT-4自动判断模型输出是否存在幻觉"] --> F["计算各模型在不同问题类别的非幻觉率"]
    F["计算各模型在不同问题类别的非幻觉率"] --> G["分析不同类型模型的主要幻觉类型及成因"]

name: HallusionBench
intro: HallusionBench是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估图像-上下文推理能力。该基准测试通过强调对视觉数据的细致理解和解释，对先进的大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）提出了重大挑战。它包含346张图像和1129个问题，全部由人类专家精心制作。通过新颖的视觉问题结构和对照组设计，HallusionBench能够对模型的响应趋势、逻辑一致性和各种故障模式进行定量分析，并深入理解语言幻觉和视觉错觉等问题。
org: University of Maryland, University of California, Beijing Institute of Technology, Tencent AI Lab, C
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
metric: Accuracy, Question Pair Acc, Figure Acc, Easy Question Acc, Hard Question Acc
modality: 图像, 文本, 多模态
intro_en: HallusionBench is a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate image-context reasoning abilities. By emphasizing detailed understanding and interpretation of visual data, it poses significant challenges to advanced large vision-language models (LVLMs). It includes 346 images and 1,129 questions, all carefully crafted by human experts. Through novel visual question structures and control group designs, HallusionBench enables quantitative analysis of model response patterns, logical consistency, and various failure modes, providing deep insights into issues such as language hallucination and visual illusions.
metric_en: Accuracy, Question Pair Acc, Figure Acc, Easy Question Acc, Hard Question Acc
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect 346 original images from the Internet covering diverse topics and visual formats"] --> B["Define Visual Dependent and Visual Supplement question taxonomies to probe language bias"]
    B["Define Visual Dependent and Visual Supplement question taxonomies to probe language bias"] --> C["Human experts craft 1129 questions paired with images as control groups"]
    C["Human experts craft 1129 questions paired with images as control groups"] --> D["Manually edit 181 images to alter correct answers while preserving question text"]
    D["Manually edit 181 images to alter correct answers while preserving question text"] --> E["Structure 455 visual-question control pairs with original and edited image variants"]
    E["Structure 455 visual-question control pairs with original and edited image variants"] --> F["Evaluate 15 LVLMs using the benchmark with question-pair accuracy metric"]
    F["Evaluate 15 LVLMs using the benchmark with question-pair accuracy metric"] --> G["Analyze model failure modes including language hallucination and visual illusion patterns"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从互联网收集346张涵盖多样主题和视觉格式的原始图像"] --> B["定义视觉依赖和视觉补充问题分类以探测语言偏见"]
    B["定义视觉依赖和视觉补充问题分类以探测语言偏见"] --> C["人类专家为图像精心制作1129个问题作为对照组"]
    C["人类专家为图像精心制作1129个问题作为对照组"] --> D["手动编辑181张图像以改变正确答案但保留问题文本"]
    D["手动编辑181张图像以改变正确答案但保留问题文本"] --> E["构建455个包含原始和编辑图像变体的视觉问题对照对"]
    E["构建455个包含原始和编辑图像变体的视觉问题对照对"] --> F["使用问题对准确率指标评估15个大型视觉语言模型"]
    F["使用问题对准确率指标评估15个大型视觉语言模型"] --> G["分析模型失败模式包括语言幻觉和视觉错觉模式"]

name: HarmBench
intro: HarmBench是一个用于自动化红队测试的标准化评估框架，旨在解决以往评估大型语言模型（LLM）恶意使用风险时缺乏统一标准的问题。该框架在设计时考虑了多个关键属性，并通过对18种红队方法和33个目标模型及防御策略的大规模比较，得出了新的见解。HarmBench的开源促进了攻击和防御方法的协同发展。
org: Center for AI Safety, University of California Berkeley, Stanford University, University of Illinois
metric: Attack Success Rate (ASR), GCG, GCG-M, GCG-T, PEZ, GBDA, UAT, AP, SFS, ZS, PAIR, TAP, TAP-T, AutoDAN
eval_feature: 对抗性, 多轮
intro_en: HarmBench is a standardized evaluation framework for automated red teaming, designed to address the lack of unified standards in assessing the malicious use risks of large language models (LLMs). The framework incorporates multiple key attributes and provides new insights through a large-scale comparison of 18 red teaming methods and 33 target models and defense strategies. The open-source nature of HarmBench promotes the collaborative development of attack and defense methods.
metric_en: Attack Success Rate (ASR), GCG, GCG-M, GCG-T, PEZ, GBDA, UAT, AP, SFS, ZS, PAIR, TAP, TAP-T, AutoDAN
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Curate 510 harmful behaviors across standard, contextual, copyright, and multimodal functional categories"] --> B["Organize behaviors into semantic categories including bioweapon, cybercrime, harassment, misinformation, and illegal acts"]
    B["Organize behaviors into semantic categories including bioweapon, cybercrime, harassment, misinformation, and illegal acts"] --> C["Apply 18 red teaming methods including GCG, AutoDAN, PAIR, and human jailbreaks to target models"]
    C["Apply 18 red teaming methods including GCG, AutoDAN, PAIR, and human jailbreaks to target models"] --> D["Generate adversarial prompts and collect model responses from 33 LLMs and defense configurations"]
    D["Generate adversarial prompts and collect model responses from 33 LLMs and defense configurations"] --> E["Evaluate responses using a robust classifier-based metric to determine refusal versus compliance"]
    E["Evaluate responses using a robust classifier-based metric to determine refusal versus compliance"] --> F["Compare attack success rates across methods, models, and defenses to yield novel insights"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["整理510个有害行为涵盖标准、情境、版权和多模态类别"] --> B["将行为组织为生物武器、网络犯罪、骚扰等语义类别"]
    B["将行为组织为生物武器、网络犯罪、骚扰等语义类别"] --> C["对目标模型应用18种红队方法如GCG和PAIR"]
    C["对目标模型应用18种红队方法如GCG和PAIR"] --> D["生成对抗提示并收集33个模型和防御配置的响应"]
    D["生成对抗提示并收集33个模型和防御配置的响应"] --> E["使用鲁棒分类器指标评估响应判断拒绝或遵从"]
    E["使用鲁棒分类器指标评估响应判断拒绝或遵从"] --> F["比较各方法、模型和防御的攻击成功率得出新见解"]

name: HEAD-QA
intro: HEAD-QA是基于西班牙医疗系统专业资格考试的多项选择问答基准，涵盖医学、护理学、心理学、化学、药学和生物学六个领域，对人类专家也极具挑战性。
eval_feature: 复杂推理，跨语言能力，专业资格考试难度
build_method_en: Manually collected, from Spanish medical professional qualification exams
eval_feature_en: Complex reasoning, cross-lingual ability, professional qualification exam difficulty
intro_en: HEAD-QA is a multiple-choice question benchmark based on the Spanish healthcare system professional qualification exam, covering six fields: medicine, nursing, psychology, chemistry, pharmacy, and biology. It is highly challenging even for human experts.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Spanish Medical Exams) --> B[Exam-based Data Collection]
    B --> C[Question Selection]
    C --> D{{Expert Construction}}
    D --> E[Answer Verification]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -- Pass --> G(Validated Q&A Pairs)
    F -- Fail --> H[Refinement/Discard]
    G --> I(HEAD-QA Dataset)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Medical Knowledge Assessment)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect multi-choice questions from Spanish healthcare system exams from 2013 to present"] --> B["Extract questions from six healthcare areas: medicine, pharmacology, psychology, nursing, biology, and chemistry"]
    B["Extract questions from six healthcare areas: medicine, pharmacology, psychology, nursing, biology, and chemistry"] --> C["Remove questions marked as invalid after official exam administration"]
    C["Remove questions marked as invalid after official exam administration"] --> D["Include images referenced in questions as part of the corpus"]
    D["Include images referenced in questions as part of the corpus"] --> E["Define official training, development, and test splits aligned with exam years"]
    E["Define official training, development, and test splits aligned with exam years"] --> F["Translate Spanish questions to English using Google API to create HEAD-QA-EN"]
    F["Translate Spanish questions to English using Google API to create HEAD-QA-EN"] --> G["Validate translation quality by sampling 60 random questions and their answers"]

    A["收集2013年至今西班牙医疗系统考试的多项选择题"] --> B["提取医学、药学、心理学、护理学、生物学和化学六个领域的问题"]
    B["提取医学、药学、心理学、护理学、生物学和化学六个领域的问题"] --> C["移除考试后被标记为无效的问题"]
    C["移除考试后被标记为无效的问题"] --> D["将问题中引用的图像纳入语料库"]
    D["将问题中引用的图像纳入语料库"] --> E["按考试年份定义官方训练集、开发集和测试集"]
    E["按考试年份定义官方训练集、开发集和测试集"] --> F["使用谷歌API将西班牙语问题翻译为英语创建HEAD-QA-EN"]
    F["使用谷歌API将西班牙语问题翻译为英语创建HEAD-QA-EN"] --> G["抽样60个随机问题及答案验证翻译质量"]

name: HealthBench
intro: HealthBench是一个开源基准，用于衡量大型语言模型在医疗领域的性能和安全性。它包含5000个多轮对话，由262名医生基于48562条独特评分标准进行评估，涵盖多种健康场景和行为维度。该基准支持模型在紧急情况、临床数据转换及全球健康等场景下的开放式真实评测。
build_method: 专家构建, LLM生成, 混合
eval_feature: 多轮, 领域特定
intro_en: HealthBench is an open-source benchmark designed to evaluate the performance and safety of large language models in the medical field. It includes 5,000 multi-turn dialogues assessed by 262 doctors based on 48,562 unique scoring criteria, covering various health scenarios and behavioral dimensions. The benchmark supports open-ended real-world evaluation of models in emergency situations, clinical data transformation, and global health contexts.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Healthcare Conversation Data) --> B[Data Collection: Multi-turn Dialogues]
    B --> C{Dialogue Selection & Filtering}
    C -->|Selected Dialogues| D[Initial Dialogue Set]
    D --> E{{Manual Annotation by 262 Doctors}}
    E --> F[Annotation Review & Refinement]
    F --> G{Quality Assurance Check}
    G -->|Pass| H(HealthBench Dataset: 5000 Multi-turn Conversations)
    G -->|Fail| I[Re-annotation/Discard]
    H --> J[LLM Performance & Safety Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Vet and select 262 physicians from 1,021 applicants using interest forms and rubric-writing tasks"] --> B["Physician advisors write prompt seeds for synthetic conversation generation across health domains"]
    B["Physician advisors write prompt seeds for synthetic conversation generation across health domains"] --> C["Generate and select 5,000 multi-turn conversations for relevance, realism, and difficulty"]
    C["Generate and select 5,000 multi-turn conversations for relevance, realism, and difficulty"] --> D["Physicians write conversation-specific rubric criteria with point values from -10 to 10"]
    D["Physicians write conversation-specific rubric criteria with point values from -10 to 10"] --> E["Assign 34 consensus criteria only when majority of reviewing physicians agree on relevance"]
    E["Assign 34 consensus criteria only when majority of reviewing physicians agree on relevance"] --> F["Model-based grader independently scores each response against all rubric criteria"]
    F["Model-based grader independently scores each response against all rubric criteria"] --> G["Sum criterion points and normalize by maximum possible score per example"]
    G["Sum criterion points and normalize by maximum possible score per example"] --> H["Create HealthBench Hard subset of 1,000 examples difficult for frontier models"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["从1021名申请者中筛选262名医生"] --> B["医生顾问编写提示种子生成对话"]
    B["医生顾问编写提示种子生成对话"] --> C["生成并选择5000个多轮对话"]
    C["生成并选择5000个多轮对话"] --> D["医生为对话编写评分标准并赋值"]
    D["医生为对话编写评分标准并赋值"] --> E["多数医生同意时分配共识标准"]
    E["多数医生同意时分配共识标准"] --> F["模型评分器对照标准独立评分"]
    F["模型评分器对照标准独立评分"] --> G["汇总标准得分并归一化"]
    G["汇总标准得分并归一化"] --> H["创建1000个困难样本子集"]

name: HealthSearchQA
intro: HealthSearchQA是Google在MultiMedQA论文中新引入的自由回答医学问题数据集，包含3173个来自健康搜索引擎的消费者医疗问题，旨在评估大型语言模型在真实用户健康查询场景中的表现。
build_method_en: Automatically generated, from health search engine queries
eval_feature_en: Evaluates LLM knowledge and reasoning ability in consumer health query scenarios
intro_en: HealthSearchQA is a free-response medical question dataset newly introduced by Google in the MultiMedQA paper. It contains 3,173 consumer health questions from health search engines and aims to evaluate large language models' performance in real-world user health query scenarios.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Web-based Medical Sources] --> B{{Web Crawling & Hybrid Collection}}
    B --> C(Raw Health Q&A Data)
    C --> D[Text Cleaning & Normalization]
    D --> E[Question-Answer Extraction]
    E --> F(Processed Q&A Pairs)
    F --> G{Medical Expert Review}
    G --"Approved"--> H[Quality Filtering]
    G --"Rejected"--> I[Discard Low Quality]
    H --> J(HealthSearchQA Dataset)
    J --> K[Model Evaluation]
    K --> L[Information Retrieval & QA Capabilities]
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect 3173 consumer medical questions from online health search engines"] --> B["Combine HealthSearchQA with six existing medical QA datasets to form MultiMedQA"]
    B["Combine HealthSearchQA with six existing medical QA datasets to form MultiMedQA"] --> C["Generate answers using PaLM and instruction-tuned Flan-PaLM with prompting strategies"]
    C["Generate answers using PaLM and instruction-tuned Flan-PaLM with prompting strategies"] --> D["Collect clinician-generated reference answers for consumer medical questions"]
    D["Collect clinician-generated reference answers for consumer medical questions"] --> E["Conduct human evaluation of model answers along factuality, precision, harm, and bias axes"]
    E["Conduct human evaluation of model answers along factuality, precision, harm, and bias axes"] --> F["Compare model-generated answers with clinician answers under the evaluation framework"]
    F["Compare model-generated answers with clinician answers under the evaluation framework"] --> G["Apply instruction prompt tuning to create Med-PaLM and re-evaluate performance"]

    B["将HealthSearchQA与六个现有医学问答数据集组合成MultiMedQA"] --> C["使用PaLM和指令调优的Flan-PaLM通过提示策略生成答案"]
    C["使用PaLM和指令调优的Flan-PaLM通过提示策略生成答案"] --> D["为消费者医疗问题收集临床医生生成的参考答案"]
    D["为消费者医疗问题收集临床医生生成的参考答案"] --> E["沿事实性、精确性、危害性和偏见轴对模型答案进行人工评估"]
    E["沿事实性、精确性、危害性和偏见轴对模型答案进行人工评估"] --> F["在评估框架下比较模型生成答案与临床医生答案"]
    F["在评估框架下比较模型生成答案与临床医生答案"] --> G["应用指令提示调优创建Med-PaLM并重新评估性能"]

name: HellaSwag
intro: HellaSwag是一个常识推理挑战数据集。人类在该数据集上的准确率超过95%，但先进的语言模型得分却很低。该数据集通过对抗性过滤（Adversarial Filtering）方法构建，旨在生成对机器而言极具挑战性、但对人类来说却很简单的句子补全问题。
org: University of Washington, Allen Institute for AI
build_method: LLM生成, 对抗性过滤
eval_feature: 对抗性, 常识推理
intro_en: HellaSwag is a commonsense reasoning challenge dataset. Humans achieve over 95% accuracy on it, while advanced language models score much lower. The dataset is constructed using Adversarial Filtering to create sentence completion tasks that are very challenging for machines but easy for humans.
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Collect high-quality source text for event descriptions and contexts"] --> B["Scale context to three sentences and generate two-sentence continuations using state-of-the-art generators"]
    B["Scale context to three sentences and generate two-sentence continuations using state-of-the-art generators"] --> C["Apply discriminators iteratively to select machine-generated wrong answers that fool models"]
    C["Apply discriminators iteratively to select machine-generated wrong answers that fool models"] --> D["Filter generations to the Goldilocks zone where text is nonsensical to humans but misclassified by models"]
    D["Filter generations to the Goldilocks zone where text is nonsensical to humans but misclassified by models"] --> E["Pair each context with one correct continuation and adversarially-filtered wrong answer candidates"]
    E["Pair each context with one correct continuation and adversarially-filtered wrong answer candidates"] --> F["Validate human accuracy exceeds 95% while state-of-the-art models score below 48%"]
    F["Validate human accuracy exceeds 95% while state-of-the-art models score below 48%"] --> G["Evaluate models on 70k commonsense inference problems requiring sentence completion selection"]
flowchart_zh:
flowchart LR
    A["收集高质量源文本作为事件描述和上下文"] --> B["将上下文扩展至三句并用先进生成器生成两句续写"]
    B["将上下文扩展至三句并用先进生成器生成两句续写"] --> C["迭代应用判别器筛选能迷惑模型的机器生成错误答案"]
    C["迭代应用判别器筛选能迷惑模型的机器生成错误答案"] --> D["过滤生成文本至对人类荒谬但模型误判的黄金区间"]
    D["过滤生成文本至对人类荒谬但模型误判的黄金区间"] --> E["为每个上下文配对正确续写和对抗过滤的错误候选项"]
    E["为每个上下文配对正确续写和对抗过滤的错误候选项"] --> F["验证人类准确率超95%而先进模型得分低于48%"]
    F["验证人类准确率超95%而先进模型得分低于48%"] --> G["在7万个需要选择句子补全的常识推理问题上评估模型"]

name: HELMET
intro: HELMET是一个全面的基准测试，旨在有效且彻底地评估长上下文语言模型（LCLMs）。它涵盖了七个多样化的、以应用为中心的类别，解决了现有基准测试中存在的覆盖范围有限、上下文长度不足和评估指标不可靠等问题。HELMET通过增加可控的上下文长度（高达128K tokens）、基于模型的评估和少样本提示，提供了更可靠和一致的LCLM排名。研究发现，合成任务不能可靠预测下游性能，且HELMET中不同类别之间趋势各异、相关性低。
metric: Accuracy, Pass@k, F1, BLEU, Win Rate, Success Rate
task_type: 开放式问答, 选择题, 摘要, 信息检索, 文本生成
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Multiple Choice, Summarization, information retrieval, text generation
intro_en: HELMET is a comprehensive benchmark designed to effectively and thoroughly evaluate long-context language models (LCLMs). It covers seven diverse, application-focused categories, addressing limitations in existing benchmarks such as limited coverage, insufficient context length, and unreliable evaluation metrics. HELMET offers more reliable and consistent LCLM rankings by incorporating controllable context lengths (up to 128K tokens), model-based evaluation, and few-shot prompting. Studies show that synthetic tasks do not reliably predict downstream performance, and trends vary with low correlation across different categories within HELMET.
metric_en: Accuracy, Pass@k, F1, BLEU, Win Rate, Success Rate
flowchart_en:
flowchart LR
    A["Select QA datasets and retrieve k-1 distractor passages using gte-large-en-v1.5 from Wikipedia 2019 dump"] --> B["Insert gold passage at six positions or shuffle for multi-hop QA to create RAG tasks"]
    B["Insert gold passage at six positions or shuffle for multi-hop QA to create RAG tasks"] --> C["Prepare ALCE citation tasks with top k retrieved Wikipedia passages for answer generation with citations"]
    C["Prepare ALCE citation tasks with top k retrieved Wikipedia passages for answer generation with citations"] --> D["Construct MS MARCO re-ranking tasks with BM25-retrieved passages and relevance labels up to length L"]
    D["Construct MS MARCO re-ranking tasks with BM25-retrieved passages and relevance labels up to length L"] --> E["Build many-shot ICL tasks from TREC and intent classification datasets with few-shot prompting for base models"]
    E["Build many-shot ICL tasks from TREC and intent classification datasets with few-shot prompting for base models"] --> F["Assemble long-document QA from NarrativeQA and ∞Bench with controllable lengths up to 128K tokens"]
    F["Assemble long-document QA from NarrativeQA and ∞Bench with controllable lengths up to 128K tokens"] --> G["Create summarization tasks from ∞Bench and Multi-LexSum with extended context lengths"]
